title: [bug] HostWindow thread affinity deadlocks main thread after fullscreen
description:
## Root cause (proven by logs 2026-04-19 23:12:30)

HostWindow's HWND is created inside `ensure_init`, which is called from `#[tauri::command] async fn player_load_url`. Async Tauri commands run on a tokio worker thread, so the HWND is thread-affine to that tokio worker.

Win32 SetWindowPos called cross-thread does SendMessage to the owner thread and waits for the owner to dispatch it. Tokio workers don't pump Win32 messages, so the call blocks indefinitely.

During normal drag-resize this is sporadically OK (the worker thread occasionally gets awakened by tokio activity that happens to service the queue), but the fullscreen path in `player_set_fullscreen`'s `run_on_main_thread` closure deadlocks reliably: the main thread goes into SetWindowPos and never returns. Evidence: `set_geometry` logs fire, but the follow-up `sync_geometry returned` log never does.

With the main thread stuck, Tauri's IPC handler stops processing. When React's cleanup invokes `player_unload`, the command queues but never reaches the backend — explaining the 'audio continues 20s, no [player:cmd] unload in log' report.

## Fix
Own the HostWindow on the main thread. `ensure_init` dispatches HostWindow creation via `app.run_on_main_thread` and a one-shot channel to wait for the result. `destroy` dispatches DestroyWindow the same way. SetWindowPos from any thread to a main-thread-owned window works cleanly because main always pumps messages.

## Acceptance
- Toggle fullscreen, click back → audio cuts immediately and `[player:cmd] unload` appears in log
- Drag-resize still works (same behavior as today)
- No 'cursor is loading spinner' regression

## Required Skills
rust, tauri, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Resolved on feature/native-player. u4c: HostWindow now owned on main thread (f5bc7bc) — fullscreen + un-fullscreen confirmed working. 31n: suspected commits 252b631/d2349ee/fee7d2e were reverted; safer re-application landed in 6f937e1 and HostWindow main-thread fix; user confirmed nav/play/pause work without unresponsive window.

title: [bug] Native player: main window unresponsive on first Player load (regression)
description:
## Symptom
After my 3 commits (252b631, d2349ee, fee7d2e), launching the app and navigating to the Player:
- Video plays (mpv renders, audio audible)
- Tauri main window does not accept focus
- Windows shows the busy/wait cursor (IDC_APPSTARTING / IDC_WAIT) over the app
- Keyboard input goes to the terminal, not the app (Esc cancels shell input)
- Only escape hatch is Ctrl+C on the terminal

Logs confirm:
- FileLoaded → player://ready fires normally
- time-pos progresses (video is decoding)
- No errors or warnings from player subsystems
- No set_fullscreen command ever fires — user can't trigger it

## Suspected causes (in order of likelihood)

1. **252b631 (destroy joins event pump)**: handle.join() blocks the calling thread. If something during the StrictMode double-mount interleaves destroy() with a cross-thread SetWindowPos from the Tauri UI thread targeting our host HWND (owned by a tokio worker), main would SendMessage-block while the worker is blocked on join(). But destroy should be a no-op on first load (Inner=None) so this shouldn't actually fire.
2. **d2349ee (WM_SIZE → async child resize)**: new WndProc logic that runs whenever WM_SIZE hits our host. The SWP_ASYNCWINDOWPOS flag should make it non-blocking, but re-entrancy or an unforeseen message path could stall something.
3. **fee7d2e (cleanup chain)**: cleanup now chains unload → fullscreen-exit. A fire-and-forget Promise chain in useEffect cleanup shouldn't block the JS thread, and the Rust side is independent.

## Next step
Revert all 3 commits, rebuild, retest. If the hang disappears, bisect to find the specific regression.

## Required Skills
rust, tauri, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Resolved on feature/native-player. u4c: HostWindow now owned on main thread (f5bc7bc) — fullscreen + un-fullscreen confirmed working. 31n: suspected commits 252b631/d2349ee/fee7d2e were reverted; safer re-application landed in 6f937e1 and HostWindow main-thread fix; user confirmed nav/play/pause work without unresponsive window.

title: [bug] Native player: mpv D3D11 swapchain doesn't resize on fullscreen
description:
## Root cause

The host HWND correctly resizes to display dimensions (3840x2160) during fullscreen — confirmed in commit 8196e41 and the beads memory. `SetWindowPos` in `HostWindow::set_geometry` succeeds. But mpv's internal D3D11 swapchain stays at the pre-fullscreen size.

In `src-tauri/src/player/host_window.rs:30-37`, the WndProc is just `DefWindowProcW`. mpv creates a child render surface inside our HWND (confirmed by earlier working fix at commit b261182, which found a child via `GetWindow(hwnd, GW_CHILD)`). When the parent HWND resizes, WM_SIZE fires on the parent, but child windows in Win32 do NOT auto-resize — the parent must explicitly move children. Our WndProc swallows WM_SIZE into DefWindowProcW with no child handling.

Previous fix b261182 added WM_SIZE → MoveWindow on GW_CHILD inside our WndProc. This was reverted in 30a4949 because "SetWindowPos on mpv's child triggered a D3D11 swapchain rebuild that blocked the main thread". The PostMessage variant (`post_resize` in older code) was also removed as dead code in 193904c.

## Fix options (in order of preference)

**Option A — non-blocking child resize via SWP_ASYNCWINDOWPOS + bRepaint=false:**
Re-add WM_SIZE handler in `wnd_proc` that resizes GW_CHILD with `MoveWindow(child, 0, 0, w, h, false /* bRepaint */)` or `SetWindowPos(..., SWP_ASYNCWINDOWPOS | SWP_NOREDRAW)`. The async flag posts the resize to mpv's thread's queue instead of sending synchronously, avoiding the main-thread block that motivated 30a4949.

**Option B — tell mpv to reconfigure:**
After sync_geometry applies a large jump (post-fullscreen), invoke an mpv command like `mpv.set_property("window-scale", 1.0)` or similar to force it to re-read window size. Lower risk of deadlock but depends on whether libmpv exposes a reconfigure trigger we can call off-thread.

**Option C — subclass + explicit swapchain size:**
Use `SetWindowSubclass` on mpv's child HWND to intercept its events. More invasive; only if A/B fail.

## Test plan
- Enter fullscreen during playback: video fills the entire screen (not just the pre-fullscreen region)
- Exit fullscreen: video returns to the window region
- Enter→exit→enter cycle: each transition correctly resizes (memory note says 2nd play has issues)
- Drag-resize the window: video follows smoothly
- No main-thread freeze during transition

## Required Skills
rust, tauri, prexu, anti-fabrication

## History
- b261182 added child resize — worked but blocked main thread
- 0e0eda8 tried SWP_ASYNCWINDOWPOS — still flaky
- 30a4949 removed child resize entirely — current state, video doesn't resize
- 193904c deleted the SWP_ASYNCWINDOWPOS constant + post_resize as dead code
close_reason: WndProc now handles WM_SIZE and resizes GW_CHILD with SWP_ASYNCWINDOWPOS + NOREDRAW flags. Async flag posts the resize to mpv's thread queue, avoiding the main-thread block that killed the earlier sync attempt. Commit d2349ee.

title: [bug] Native player: drop-order race crashes mpv on destroy
description:
## Root cause

In `src-tauri/src/player/mod.rs`, `Inner` declares fields in order: `mpv: Arc<Mpv>` first, `host: HostWindow` second. Rust drops struct fields in declaration order, so when `destroy()` runs `*guard = None;`:

1. `Arc<Mpv>` drops → refcount 2→1 (event pump thread still holds the other Arc). `mpv_terminate_destroy` is NOT called yet.
2. `HostWindow` drops → `DestroyWindow` runs on the HWND that mpv's render thread is still using.
3. Much later, the event pump's `wait_event(1.0)` returns `Event::Shutdown`, loop breaks, its Arc drops (1→0), `mpv_terminate_destroy` finally runs — but the HWND is already gone.

The window between step 2 and step 3 is the crash window: mpv's D3D11 render thread tries to present to a destroyed HWND. Verified against libmpv2 source at `~/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-.../libmpv2-4.1.0/src/mpv.rs:465-468` (Drop calls `mpv_terminate_destroy`).

The existing code comment on `Inner.host` (mod.rs:56-61) is aspirational — the field order is correct in intent but drops aren't synchronous because another Arc is live.

## Also explains: audio keeps playing after navigating to dashboard

`player_unload` returns to JS as soon as `destroy()` returns — which is BEFORE mpv has actually terminated. mpv's audio output keeps feeding the device until the event pump processes Shutdown (up to ~1s due to the `wait_event(1.0)` timeout plus whatever mpv takes internally).

## Fix

Store the event pump's `JoinHandle` on `Inner`. In `destroy()`:
1. Send `stop` + `quit` to mpv.
2. Drop/replace the Arc held on the main side first (via `take()` + `drop()`).
3. `join()` the event pump thread — this returns after it drops its Arc.
4. NOW both Arcs are gone → `mpv_terminate_destroy` ran synchronously during the final Arc drop.
5. `HostWindow` drops cleanly.

After this, `player_unload` only returns to JS once mpv is fully dead + HWND destroyed. Audio stops before navigation happens.

## Acceptance
- Navigate to dashboard while playing: audio stops within one frame
- Close app while playing: no crash in logs
- Repeated play→dashboard→play cycles: no zombie mpv state

## Required Skills
rust, tauri, prexu, anti-fabrication
notes: Found during thorough app review 2026-04-19. Related to beads memory 'native-player-fullscreen-host-window-geometry-sync-confirmed'.
close_reason: Event pump JoinHandle stored in Inner; destroy() takes Inner out, sends stop+quit, joins pump, then drops. Final Arc drop runs mpv_terminate_destroy synchronously before HostWindow::drop. Commit 252b631.

title: [bug] Main window restores to wrong size after maximize→unmaximize
description:
REPRO (dashboard, no player open):
1. Launch app (opens at configured 1280x800-ish).
2. Resize window wider/taller to size B.
3. Click OS maximize (square) button — fills screen as expected.
4. Click maximize/restore again to un-maximize.
EXPECTED: restores to size B (pre-maximize geometry).
ACTUAL: restores to a hybrid — launch HEIGHT but a width narrower than B. Not the pre-maximize rect.

NOTES:
- Separate from the native-player/popout path (Player overlay not mounted on dashboard; popout SetWindowPos code does not run here).
- Main window uses tauri-plugin-window-state (window-state:default). Suspect the plugin's persisted inner-size restore is racing/overriding the OS restore rect, possibly compounded by per-monitor DPI (user on 4K secondary, work area origin negative).
- lib.rs WindowEvent::Resized handler fires window://resized + sync_geometry on maximize/restore/fullscreen; verify it isn't stashing/replaying a stale rect.

INVESTIGATION START: tauri-plugin-window-state registration in lib.rs; whether StateFlags include SIZE/POSITION and when it saves (on every resize?). Try excluding maximized state or deferring restore.

3. Click maximize again to un-maximize.

title: [bug] video doesn't render — html element painted opaque navy blocks transparency chain to mpv host
description:
Regression from prexu-vs5 fix. The inline CSS added to index.html in commit d1768ce60 styled BOTH html and body opaque navy: 'html, body { background-color: #1a1a2e; margin: 0; }'. The useTransparentWindow hook from prexu-r3l toggles 'body.player-transparent' to make body transparent during a native player session — but it does NOT touch the html element. html stays opaque navy. Since the layer stack is html → body → root → AppLayout, and mpv host renders BEHIND the WebView, EVERY layer must be alpha=0 for mpv to show through. html opaque navy blocks the chain.

Reproducer: start any video. Audio plays, video doesn't render — user sees navy where video should be. Logs confirm mpv is genuinely playing (hwdec=d3d11va, time-pos advancing, FileLoaded fired) but the pixels can't reach the user through the WebView.

Fix: drop 'html' from the inline CSS selector. html stays transparent (its default). body retains the opaque-navy default and is toggled via the .player-transparent class as designed.

The prexu-vs5 cold-start protection still works because the primary defense is Tauri's 'visible: false' + the app_ready handshake — the window doesn't show until React has painted. The inline body CSS is belt-and-suspenders for the body element specifically; html doesn't need it (and shouldn't have it).

## Required Skills
react, prexu, tauri, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Fixed. Dropped 'html' from the inline opaque-navy selector in index.html. html stays transparent (its default), body retains its toggle-via-class behavior. The transparency chain html→body→root→AppLayout now reaches mpv host behind the WebView. tsc + 1387 tests clean. Vite hot-reload will pick this up automatically — refresh the WebView and re-test playback.

title: [bug] applySubtitleStyle IPC cascade — fires every 250ms time-pos tick → mpv video output stalls
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-24 during VS5.2 playback testing on feature/native-player. Symptom: video plays audio but no picture (just navy bg in the middle of the player chrome). Logs show [player:cmd] apply_sub_style firing every time-pos tick (~4Hz), which translates to ~24 mpv property writes per second on the Tauri main thread. mpv's gpu-next vo can't maintain its D3D11 swapchain output through that traffic so video stalls (audio is on a separate thread so it survives). Post-stop, the same effect fires once more after player_unload destroyed mpv, producing 'apply_sub_style failed mpv not initialised' in the React console.

Root cause introduced in prexu-ve9 (commit 45472b20e): src/pages/Player.tsx L82-84 has the dep array [player, pb.subtitleSize, pb.subtitleStyle]. The player object is a useMemo inside useNativePlayer with currentTime as one of its deps — so every time-pos tick produces a new player object identity, the subtitle-style useEffect re-fires, and the IPC pumps. Pre-prexu-ve9 the dep was [player.isLoading, ...] which is binary (false→true once) so the effect ran once on ready.

Fix: depend on the stable useCallback identity by destructuring applySubtitleStyle from player and using that in the dep list. applySubtitleStyle itself is wrapped in useCallback inside useNativePlayer with stable deps so its identity survives re-renders.

## Required Skills
react, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Fixed. Player.tsx subtitle-style useEffect dep changed from [player, ...] to [applySubtitleStyle, ...] where applySubtitleStyle is destructured from player. applySubtitleStyle is a useCallback([], []) inside useNativePlayer with stable identity; player is a useMemo with currentTime in its deps so it churned every time-pos tick. With the fix the effect fires only on actual subtitleStyle/subtitleSize pref change. Restart dev session, refresh after rebuild, and re-test playback — video should render normally now. All 1387 tests pass, tsc clean. Other [player, ...] deps in the codebase (handleAudioEnhancementChange, usePlayerLifecycle exit/exitFullscreenIfActive, useRemotePlayback syncSeek) are useCallback definitions not useEffect — they churn callback identity but don't pump IPC; leaving alone for now.

title: [bug] PosterCard renders <button> inside <button> → React hydration loop → WebView2 OOM crash
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-24 during testing on feature/native-player. After dashboard loads, the page freezes for ~5s then shows WebView2's 'This page is having a problem' error overlay (Out of Memory). DevTools console shows:

  In HTML, <button> cannot be a descendant of <button>.
  This will cause a hydration error.
  e <button> cannot contain a nested <button>.

Reproducer: open the app cold, wait for dashboard, observe freeze + crash. Also re-triggers when clicking the > arrow to scroll new OnDeck tiles into view.

Root cause: src/components/PosterCard.tsx L84 renders the entire card as <button className="card-enter">, and at L146/168/197 nests three more <button> elements inside it (More options menu, Play, Expand). HTML spec forbids button-in-button; browsers DOM-fixup by hoisting the inner buttons out of the outer, which breaks React's reconciler. React thrashes re-rendering, runs out of heap, WebView2 renderer crashes.

Fix: change the outer <button className="card-enter"> to a <div role="button" tabIndex={0} onKeyDown={...}> that preserves keyboard activation but is a valid container for nested interactive elements. The inner buttons stay as real <button>s.

Pre-existing problem (not introduced by recent work) — likely got more aggressive lately because newer React 19 is stricter about hydration mismatches.

PosterCard's outer <button className='card-enter'> changed to <div role='button' tabIndex={0} onKeyDown=...> so the nested More/Play/Expand <button>s no longer violate the HTML 'no button-in-button' rule. title: [bug] mask-position calc(100% - Npx) interprets % differently than expected, hole misplaced
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-21 via DOM-verified user testing. AppLayout's mask uses calc(100% - 376px) and calc(100% - 216px) for the second mask layer's position. The intent was 'hole 16px from right + bottom edges with 360x200 dims'. The actual CSS spec rule for background-position / mask-position percentages is offset = (positioning_area − image_size) × percentage. So calc(100% - 376px) resolves to (W − 360) − 376 = W − 736, placing the hole one full hole-width to the LEFT and one full hole-height ABOVE the bottom-right corner where the mpv host actually lives. The user observed the hole rendering in the middle of the content area between the 'Recently Added' rows, while mpv stayed at the actual bottom-right corner.

Fix: use the four-value mask-position syntax 'right 16px bottom 16px' which anchors mask edges to positioning-area edges, matching the bottom:16/right:16 semantics of miniContainer in Player.tsx exactly. No DPI dependence — purely a position correction.

## Required Skills
react, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Fixed in 965173cd. mask-position now uses 'right 16px bottom 16px' (four-value edge-anchored syntax) instead of 'calc(100% - 376px) calc(100% - 216px)' (which percentage-rule-resolves incorrectly to (W−360)−376). Hole will now anchor to bottom-right corner with 16px gutter, exactly matching miniContainer's bottom:16/right:16. mpv host at the same coordinates should be visible through it.

title: [bug] minimize inset misaligned at non-100% DPI: Rust uses physical px, CSS mask uses logical px
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-21 during prexu-s0f testing.

When player_enter_minimize is invoked with (width=360, height=200, padding=16), Rust stores those values verbatim in state.minimize and apply_minimize_inset uses them as PHYSICAL pixel offsets against inner_size (which Tauri reports in physical px). On a 4K monitor at 150%/200% DPI scale, the mpv host gets placed at (inner_w_phys - 376, ...) physical px.

Meanwhile AppLayout's CSS mask uses  where 100% = the container's CSS-pixel width (logical). At DPI 2x, CSS 376 = physical 752. So the mask hole is positioned 752 physical px from the right edge while the mpv host is 376 physical px from the right edge. The hole reveals the empty area NEXT TO mpv, not mpv itself.

Fix: scale the user-supplied CSS units to physical in Rust via main.scale_factor() before storing in state.minimize. The mpv host ends up at the same physical position the CSS mask occupies after WebView2's logical-to-physical conversion.

Future (7il.5/7il.7): when the minimize size/corner becomes user-configurable, the values stay in CSS units on the React side (where the user actually sees them at consistent visual size regardless of DPI). Rust handles the conversion every time.

Additional follow-up: when corner becomes draggable (7il.7), AppLayout mask needs to read the actual corner from PlayerContext so the hole tracks the chrome. For now (bottom-right only), hardcoded calc is fine.

## Required Skills
rust, prexu, anti-fabrication

Body is transparent. AppLayout fills 100vh as expected. User's scale_factor=1.00 made the DPI fix a no-op on their setup but it remains correct for >1.0 setups. Image #24 shows a dark rectangular blob in the bottom-right at the expected hole position with MiniChrome close-X at its corner; the dark interior is mpv playing the cold open of Alice in Borderland S01E01 (starts at offset 0). title: [bug] AppLayout's opaque pixels cover mpv host in minimize mode (need CSS mask hole)
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-21 during prexu-ya6 testing.

With prexu-4k5 + prexu-ya6 fixes, AppLayout now paints opaquely across the viewport during minimize mode. But mpv on Windows is a SIBLING Win32 window BEHIND the WebView — for the mpv host to be visible, the WebView pixels in the mini region must be transparent so the OS composites the Win32 mpv window through. AppLayout's opaque main/header/sidebar in the bottom-right corner blocks this.

Fix: apply CSS mask-composite:exclude on AppLayout's container when isMinimized, with two mask layers — full viewport (100% 100% at 0 0) and a 360x200 rect at calc(100%-376px) calc(100%-216px). The exclude composite punches a hole the exact size + position of the miniContainer. Body needs to stay transparent (revert prexu-4k5) so the hole shows through to the WebView's transparent pixels and the Win32 mpv host behind. MiniChrome stays on top because it lives outside AppLayout in the DOM (PlayerOverlay sibling).

When 7il.5 (resizable minimize) lands, mask coordinates will need to track the resized size — easy follow-up via CSS variables.

react, prexu, anti-fabrication

Player.tsx body stays transparent throughout (prexu-4k5 reverted) so the hole reveals truly transparent WebView pixels and the OS composites the Win32 mpv host through. title: [bug] AppLayout visibility:hidden in player-session hides the app even in minimize mode
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-21 during prexu-4k5 testing.

AppLayout.tsx around line 106 sets visibility:hidden on its root container whenever playerSession.session != null. The intent (prexu-kfa) was to keep mpv's HostWindow (Win32 sibling behind the WebView2) unobscured while the player is full-viewport — AppLayout's opaque header/sidebar/main would otherwise occlude the video.

That logic doesn't account for in-window minimize mode (7il.3). When isMinimized, only a small 360x200 bottom-right region is the mpv host area; AppLayout should be VISIBLE across the rest of the viewport so the user can see + interact with the underlying app (the whole point of minimize). Currently AppLayout stays hidden and the user just sees navy body + MiniChrome, no Prexu app.

Fix: gate playerActive on session AND !isMinimized:

  const playerActive = playerSession.session != null && !playerSession.isMinimized;

When minimized, AppLayout renders normally with opaque content. miniContainer's own background:transparent in the small region lets the WebView pixels pass through to the mpv host behind, so the video still shows in the corner. AppLayout covers the rest.

When minimized, AppLayout renders normally (header/sidebar/main visible), miniContainer's transparent background still lets WebView pixels through to the mpv host behind in the small bottom-right region. This implements the user's stop-playback analogy: same body-opaque behaviour, but AppLayout stays in the DOM and painted, just like it would be after stop.

title: [bug] body kept transparent in minimize mode hides route layer, desktop wallpaper shows through
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-21 during prexu-70y testing.

Player.tsx's useLayoutEffect (~line 127) sets document.body.style.background = 'transparent' on mount of the native player path. The trick was originally for full-viewport mode: body is transparent so the mpv Win32 sibling window (BEHIND the WebView) shows through. Made sense when Player.tsx always covered 100vh.

Now in 7il.3 minimize mode, Player.tsx renders only a small 360x200 bottom-right region. But the body stays transparent everywhere else. Tauri main window has transparent:true, so the WebView outside the mini region is see-through to the OS desktop wallpaper — defeating the whole point of minimize mode (showing the Prexu app behind the small player).

Fix: gate the body transparency on !playerSession.isMinimized. In full-viewport player mode, body stays transparent as before. title: [bug] minimize state leaks across popout transition, leaving host stuck at corner inset
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-21 during prexu-70y testing. Two visible symptoms with the same root cause:

1. Minimize → click Pop-out: pop-out window renders at correct size, but the mpv host is stuck in the bottom-right inset position INSIDE the small pop-out window.
2. After (1) happens, exiting pop-out leaves state.minimize=Some stranded. The full chrome renders (React.isMinimized=false) but mpv host stays at the inset, so resizing the main window leaves a small video in the bottom-right with the chrome wrapping around it.

Root cause: in src/pages/Player.tsx the togglePiP useCallback fires playerExitMinimize and playerEnterPopOut in parallel via Promise.then chains. They race on the tokio runtime. During player_enter_popout's synchronous SetWindowPos storm the Resized events read state.minimize via sync_geometry; if exit_minimize hasn't won the race yet, the inset is applied to the new (popout-sized) inner rect.

Fix: Rust-side authoritative — player_enter_popout clears state.minimize at the start of the command. Pop-out and minimize are mutually exclusive (7il.4 design) so this is correct semantics. Eliminates race because once state.minimize = None, any concurrent Resized events skip apply_minimize_inset.

## Required Skills
rust, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Fixed in d9e9aea. player_enter_popout now clears state.minimize at the start of the command, before any geometry work. Race is eliminated: even if playerExitMinimize hasn't run yet on the React side, by the time enter_popout's SetWindowPos storm fires Resized events the inset is already None, so apply_minimize_inset is pass-through. cargo check clean.

Fix: have apply_host_topmost accept Some(parent) on the false path too and call host.anchor_below(parent). Update player_exit_popout to pass main.hwnd().

title: [bug] Native player: cleanup fires fullscreen + unload in parallel
description:
## Root cause

In `src/hooks/player/useNativePlayer.ts:308-318`, the useEffect cleanup on unmount fires two Tauri invocations without ordering:

```ts
if (isFullscreenRef.current) {
  invoke("player_set_fullscreen", { fullscreen: false }).catch(() => {});
}
invoke("player_unload").catch(() => {});
```

`player_set_fullscreen` on the backend (`src-tauri/src/player/commands.rs:159`) takes ~350ms (it sleeps to wait for the Win11 transition animation). `player_unload` calls `destroy()` which sends `stop` + `quit` to mpv and drops Inner.

These two commands race. The unload runs immediately after — so mpv is destroyed while the fullscreen command is still mid-transition. Then at t+350ms the fullscreen closure runs:

```rust
st.sync_geometry(x, y, w, h);  // calls inner.host.set_geometry on a dropped inner → no-op
win_for_sync.set_focus();
```

The `last_geometry` mutex is reset to None for an Inner that no longer exists. `sync_geometry` then can't find Inner via `try_lock + as_ref` and silently no-ops. That part is benign but the overall sequence means the main window may finish the fullscreen exit AFTER the dashboard has already rendered, causing the dashboard to briefly appear fullscreen.

## Additional concern: errors are silently swallowed

Both `.catch(() => {})` calls swallow all backend errors without logging. If `player_unload` fails (e.g. lock poisoned), audio keeps playing and nothing is logged.

## Fix

- In the cleanup, don't await (can't in useEffect), but chain: invoke fullscreen → on resolve OR timeout, invoke unload. Or simpler: since destroy() is about to tear down mpv, skip the mpv-side fullscreen call entirely and just toggle the Tauri main window fullscreen off via a dedicated command that does NOT touch mpv state.
- Replace `.catch(() => {})` with `.catch(err => logger.error("player", "cleanup_failed", ...))`.

## Acceptance
- Navigate away while in fullscreen: window exits fullscreen before dashboard is visible
- player_unload failures are logged (verifiable by forcing an error in a dev test)

## Required Skills
react, tauri, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Cleanup now chains unload → fullscreen-exit. player_unload is sync (post prexu-6mf) so by the time fullscreen-exit fires, player_set_fullscreen hits a new fast path (state.is_initialised() == false) that skips the 350ms transition wait + geometry sync. Errors from both invokes are now logged via logger.error instead of silently swallowed. Commit fee7d2e.

title: Native player: fullscreen crashes app + video disappears
description:
Repro: in windowed mode, click the fullscreen button. Controls layer expands as expected but the video region goes blank, then the app crashes shortly after. Hit during Phase 2 acceptance.

Step 2.8 was minimal: useNativePlayer.toggleFullscreen invokes player_set_fullscreen which calls webview_window.set_fullscreen(true). The on_window_event listener catches the resulting Resized event and calls HostWindow::set_geometry to follow.

Likely failure modes:
1. Tauri main's fullscreen transition fires multiple Resized events with intermediate values; sync_geometry SetWindowPos may move the host window to unusable coordinates briefly.
2. Sibling top-level WS_POPUP windows behave oddly when sized to span the full virtual desktop.
3. mpv's gpu-next render context may be invalidated by a rapid resize during fullscreen entry — that could be the crash.
4. Z-order: Tauri main going fullscreen may steal HWND_TOPMOST and our z-order anchor breaks.

Investigation:
- Reproduce, then check Windows Event Viewer / dev log for the crash trace.
- Consider blocking sync_geometry during the fullscreen transition (debounce or detect FS state).
- Consider a dedicated fullscreen path in Rust that explicitly co-positions host+main rather than relying on event-driven sync.

Blocks Phase 4 (mini-player builds on the same window-pair manipulation). Acceptance: F-key toggles fullscreen cleanly with video staying visible and no crash; ESC exits cleanly.

To show video in Alt+Tab like Plex, mpv pixels must live INSIDE the main window's surface instead of a separate top-level window. Path B: DirectComposition + libmpv render API. Phase 0 = verify feasibility in the Tauri/wry windowed-WebView2 + mpv stack BEFORE rewriting the player rendering core. Linchpin unknown: can mpv composite behind a wry WebView2 in one window.
design:
# Alt-tab parity: in-surface mpv rendering — Phase 0 feasibility (VERIFIED)

## Problem
Win11 Alt+Tab live-captures the main window's composed surface and IGNORES DWM iconic bitmaps (confirmed prexu-6iyp: DwmSetIconicThumbnail returns set=Ok yet Alt+Tab stays black). mpv renders into a SEPARATE top-level window (WS_POPUP, hwndParent=None, z-anchored below main; transparent webview "hole" shows it via the desktop compositor). A separate top-level window is not part of the main window's DWM thumbnail -> black tile.

## Goal
Fold mpv pixels into the MAIN window's composed surface so Alt+Tab / Windows.Graphics.Capture include the video (like Plex).

## Verified findings

### DWM capture model
DWM composites a top-level window together with its CHILD HWNDs and its DirectComposition visuals into one per-window surface. Alt+Tab thumbnails and Windows.Graphics.Capture(CreateForWindow) capture that composed output -> they DO include child HWNDs and DComp visuals of the main window. WGC targets top-level windows only, not a child HWND directly.

### wry / WebView2 hosting
wry hosts WebView2 in WINDOWED mode: ICoreWebView2Controller bound to a wry-created WS_CHILD container HWND parented to the Tauri window. wry uses NO DirectComposition / visual hosting. Tauri exposes ICoreWebView2Controller (PlatformWebview::controller()) but NOT a CompositionController and no API to switch hosting mode. (wry src/webview2/mod.rs L396-424, L256-271)

### Airspace
Each pixel belongs to exactly one HWND; no cross-HWND alpha blending. A non-topmost DComp visual on a window sits BELOW child HWNDs and is clipped/occluded by them -> a DComp visual "behind" the windowed WebView2 child is NOT revealed through it. WebView2 transparent background reveals the HOST WINDOW content in the webview region, not an independent behind-the-child layer.

### libmpv render API (needed only for the DComp path, Path C)
mpv render API backends = OpenGL ("opengl") and Software ("sw") ONLY; no D3D11 backend (mpv#5979 open/unimplemented). To feed a DComp visual while keeping hardware decode: OpenGL via ANGLE + WGL_NV_DX_interop2 sharing the GL color texture with a D3D11 texture presented on a DComp visual (proven by media-kit). Software "sw" = full-res CPU readback per frame, defeats hwdec (fallback only). Render API REPLACES --wid embedding entirely (mutually exclusive). libmpv2 4.1.0 exposes RenderContext for the OpenGL path safely; software path needs raw libmpv2_sys FFI.

## Path evaluation
- Path B (mpv DComp visual BEHIND wry webview in one window): NOT FEASIBLE. Windowed WebView2 + airspace occludes it; would need visual hosting anyway. REJECTED.
- Path A (reparent mpv as WS_CHILD of main window): LOW effort candidate. See prexu-ga3x sub-issue. Keeps vo=gpu-next+wid (no render rewrite). RISK: sibling airspace/z-order between wry's WS_CHILD container and mpv WS_CHILD; user reports a prior child attempt "did not work" — must capture the exact prior failure first.
- Path C (full DComp visual hosting): HIGH effort/"proper". Fork wry / custom WebView2 host (ICoreWebView2CompositionController) + re-implement input/focus/IME/DPI scale + mpv render API (OpenGL/ANGLE + DX interop). Demonstrated only on macOS in Tauri ecosystem; no Windows precedent.

## Recommendation
Re-examine Path A first but reconcile with the user's "child window didn't work" report (capture exact failure). If Path A's sibling-airspace is unworkable, Path C is the only true fix and is a large project. Path B is dead.

## Sources
mpv render.h/render_gl.h; mpv#5979; WGL_NV_DX_interop2 (Khronos); media-kit#296; libmpv2 4.1.0 docs; wry src/webview2/mod.rs (commit 2f768ae); tauri PlatformWebview docs; MS DComp basic-concepts + CreateTargetForHwnd; DWM thumbnail-ovw; WebView2 DefaultBackgroundColor + windowed-vs-visual-hosting; tauri discussion #6343; wry #1540/#650(closed); WebView2Feedback #526.

title: Report final stopped timeline from Rust on window close during playback

Approach: on Tauri window close-requested during active playback, have Rust fire GET {serverUri}/:/timeline?ratingKey=..&key=/library/metadata/{rk}&state=stopped&time=..&duration=..&X-Plex-Client-Identifier=..&X-Plex-Token=.. using last-known position (frontend can push position to Rust state via the existing timeline refs, or Rust can read mpv time-pos before destroy). Must complete (or be given a short grace window) before app exit.

title: [bug] skip-credits while paused: seek lands at duration but no eof event, autoplay never triggers, player stuck
description:
## Observed (during prexu-7fe retest, 2026-05-25 session)

User paused the video, then clicked 'Skip Credits'. Skip handler seeked to the end of the file (duration). User then clicked Play. mpv stayed idle — no playback resumed, no eof-reached event fired, no postplay overlay appeared, no autoplay handoff happened. Player was stuck until user hit Exit manually.

## Log evidence (verbatim from npm run tauri dev)

```
07:56:04 [player] pause                           <- user paused
07:56:04 [player:cmd] pause
07:56:04 [player:events] paused=true
07:56:04 [player:cmd] seek to 1391.8735713768378s   <- skip-credits seek #1
07:56:04 [player:skip] active segment transition  to 'credits-1421.42'
07:56:04 [player:cmd] seek to 1397.1100779702297s   <- seek #2
07:56:06 [player:cmd] seek to 1421.42s              <- LANDS AT DURATION (file end)
07:56:06 [player:skip] active segment transition  to null
07:56:07 [player:events] wait_event timeout         <- mpv idle
07:56:09 [player] play                              <- USER clicked play
07:56:09 [player:cmd] play
07:56:10 [player:cmd] play                          <- USER double-clicked
07:56:10 [player] play
... 7 seconds of wait_event timeouts only ...
07:56:17 [player] handleExit start                   <- USER gave up, hit Exit
```

Compare to a working natural-EOF case (07:57:05 in same log):
```
07:57:05 [player:events] eof-reached=true → player://eof
07:57:05 [player] received eof event
07:57:05 [postplay] EOF reached
```
The eof-reached observer fires when *playback* reaches duration, NOT when a *seek* lands at duration.

## Root cause

mpv's `eof-reached` property goes true only when the playback engine consumes the final frame during play. A seek-to-duration while paused puts the playhead at the end but eof-reached does not flip. When the user then issues play, mpv has nothing to render (already at duration boundary), no time-pos advances, and no eof event re-emits.

Result: the postplay overlay code path (which fires on the eof event from the Rust side) never runs. No autoplay, no quit, no recovery — player is silently stuck.

## Fix candidates

**(A) Skip-credits TS handler: clamp target.** Instead of seeking to exactly `duration` (1421.42s in the example), clamp to `duration - 0.5s`. Playback then rolls naturally to the end and emits eof-reached. Smallest change. Matches the natural-EOF code path exactly. Likely lives in src/hooks/player/useSkipSegments.ts or wherever the skip-credits seek is dispatched.

**(B) Skip-credits TS handler: synthesize the eof event.** When seek target ≥ duration - epsilon, skip the seek + directly fire the postplay/eof handler. Bypasses mpv entirely for this case. More moving parts; option (A) is preferred.

**(C) Rust events: defensive eof emit on play-while-at-end.** If a play command arrives and time-pos ≥ duration - epsilon, emit player://eof immediately. Belt-and-braces but adds Rust-side state — option (A) avoids it.

Lean **(A)**.

## Test plan

- Manual: pause mid-credits → click Skip Credits → click Play → postplay overlay appears, autoplay countdown / Play Now button works
- Manual: same while UNpaused (control case — already works per natural-EOF log)
- Manual: skip-intro while paused (different segment type, same code path — verify both still seek-then-resume correctly without overshooting credits boundary)
- Manual: very short file where duration is small (< 2s of credits) — clamp epsilon shouldn't push the seek inside the credits region; the existing target IS the credits-end timestamp, so clamping back by 0.5s is fine
- Unit: skip-credits handler test — assert seek target = min(creditsEnd, duration - 0.5)

## Related

- prexu-7fe (parent) — mpv reuse epic that surfaced this during testing
- Not directly caused by 7fe — same bug would exist on the pre-7fe destroy+init path. But the soft-stop handoff working correctly is what made this gap visible (user expected fast handoff, got nothing).

## Suggested Skills

- **react**: skip-credits handler in src/hooks/player/
- **rust**: only if going with fix (C); otherwise no Rust touch needed
close_reason: Additional fix: handleSkipSegment now force-resumes play on credits skips when paused. Combined with the clamp, paused users now reach postplay automatically.

title: [bug] autoplay handoff: next episode loads paused because mpv pause property persists across loadfile
description:
## Observed (during prexu-7fe retest, 2026-05-25 session)

After autoplay handoff between episodes via the new player_stop soft-stop path, the next episode loads but stays paused. User must click play manually to start playback.

## Log evidence (verbatim from npm run tauri dev)

```
07:57:05 [player:events] eof-reached=true → player://eof
07:57:05 [player] received eof event
07:57:05 [postplay] EOF reached
07:57:05 [player:cmd] pause              <- EOF handler in TS pauses mpv
07:57:08 [player:cmd] stop                <- soft-stop fires on ratingKey change
07:57:08 [player] stop_playback: clearing current file (keep mpv alive)
07:57:08 [player:cmd] load_url offset=0ms
07:57:08 [player] ensure_init: already initialized
07:57:08 [player:cmd] load_url: ensure_init OK, sending loadfile
07:57:08 [player:events] FileLoaded → player://ready
... 30s of wait_event timeouts, mpv idle ...
07:57:39 [player:cmd] play                <- USER manually clicked play
07:57:39 [player:events] paused=false
```

## Root cause

The mpv `pause` property persists across `loadfile replace`. The EOF flow in TS sets pause=true before postplay shows. When the user picks the next episode (or autoplay fires), soft-stop runs but the prexu-7fe implementation **deliberately does NOT touch pause** — the design comment in src-tauri/src/player/mod.rs stop_playback() reads:

> Deliberately does NOT set pause=true (unlike destroy()). pause persists across loadfile replace, so flipping it on here would leave the next episode paused at start.

The reasoning was correct for setting pause=true, but the inverse case (clearing a pre-existing pause=true that was set earlier in the lifecycle) was missed. Next loadfile inherits paused=true from the prior file.

## Fix

In src-tauri/src/player/mod.rs stop_playback(), set `pause=false` (idempotent — does nothing if already false). This guarantees the next loadfile starts unpaused regardless of how the prior file ended.

Equivalent alternative: TS load_url path could set pause=false after the FileLoaded event fires. Rust-side is preferred because it keeps the contract ("soft-stop leaves mpv ready for the next loadfile") complete in one place.

## Test plan

- Manual: watch ep → let EOF fire → click 'Play Now' on postplay → next ep starts playing immediately (no manual play click needed)
- Manual: same in mini and pop-out modes
- Manual: with autoplay enabled, EOF → countdown → next ep auto-plays without intervention
- Unit: extend the existing stop_playback no-op test; add a state-machine test if feasible (mpv property assertions need a real handle, current convention skips them)

## Related

- prexu-7fe (parent) — mpv reuse on episode handoff
- prexu-may (sibling) — autoplay handoff black frame, also handoff-path bug

## Suggested Skills

- **rust**: mpv property API in stop_playback
- **react**: verify TS EOF handler still needs to pause (probably yes — pauses for postplay countdown overlay)
- **tauri**: command lifecycle around handoff
close_reason: Additional fix in player_load_url after loadfile — sets pause=false on the live new-file context, surviving the stop+loadfile cycle. Combined with the earlier stop_playback set, the unpause is now belt-and-braces.

title: player: reuse mpv instance on episode handoff (loadfile instead of destroy+init)
description:
Follow-up from prexu-may (fix A).

Current handoff path: TS player_unload → Rust destroy (stops mpv, joins event pump, drops HostWindow + Inner) → next episode triggers ensure_init (new HostWindow, new Mpv, new event pump).

Fix A made this correct in mini mode by sizing the new host to the inset on its first set_geometry. But the whole destroy+recreate cycle is still:
- Slow: full mpv terminate + new mpv init + hwdec probe again per episode
- Risky: any future bug in HostWindow init / DXGI swap chain init can resurface visual glitches like prexu-may

Fix B: don't destroy mpv on episode handoff. In player_load_url, when inner is already Some, just call mpv loadfile with the new URL + headers. ensure_init becomes a true no-op when initialized.

Open questions / risks:
1. Sub/audio track state — embedded sids reset on loadfile, but external subs (sub-add) accumulate. Need to clear them or re-add per episode.
2. audio-delay, af chain, sub-style — set via separate commands. Do they need re-applying on loadfile or do they persist across files?
3. http-header-fields — set BEFORE loadfile in current code. Same flow works.
4. start_offset_ms — already handled via loadfile's per-file opts arg, no change needed.
5. mpv property dump on cross-episode loadfile: does any state leak (e.g. paused-for-cache flag, OSD level)?

Implementation sketch:
- player_load_url: state.ensure_init() (no-op if alive) → state.with_mpv(|m| ...) same as today
- player_unload: call state.destroy(&app) ONLY on player teardown (exit player → dashboard), not between episodes
- Frontend: useNativePlayer cleanup invokes player_unload on unmount only; episode-change triggers player_load_url directly without unload first (verify current flow doesn't unload between eps)

Need to verify the frontend flow first — if usePostPlay's onPlayNext today fires unload then load, we change that to load-only. If it already just loads, the bug was on Rust side and Fix B might already be free.

Suggested skills:
- rust: mpv property/event handling
- tauri: command lifecycle

Test plan:
- Manual: in-mini autoplay handoff (same as prexu-may repro) still shows correct video
- Manual: full-screen autoplay handoff faster than current (timing)
- Manual: external sub from previous ep doesn't carry to next ep
- Manual: audio delay reset between eps (currently 0 by default)
- Unit: mock the with_mpv path, assert ensure_init returns early when initialized

Priority rationale: P2 because the current code works (post fix A); the speed-up + risk-reduction is real but not blocking. Schedule when the popout/mini polish pass is done.
close_reason: All work landed. Soft-stop handoff working, autoplay handoff plays correctly first time (load_url pause=false), Skip-Credits while paused reaches postplay (clamp + force-play). User retest 2026-05-25 confirmed all flows working.

title: [bug] mini-player: autoplay handoff leaves video frame clipped/black in mini region (prexu-a6z.4)
description:
**Reproduction (A6Z.7 testing):**
1. Start a TV episode (e.g. Yu Yu Hakusho S01E02)
2. Settings: autoplay ON
3. Click Minimize → mini chrome takes over with video in corner region
4. Scrub to near end of episode → let EOF fire
5. **Observed**: autoplay handoff fires correctly — next episode (S01E03 in test) DOES load and start playing (audio + scrub bar + controls all work). BUT the video frame is mostly BLACK in the mini region with only a thin strip of actual video visible at the bottom ~1/4 of the host window. The mini chrome size is correct, the player controls are positioned correctly, but mpv's video output is not filling the host window.

User screenshot: mini chrome at upper-left, mostly black above the scrub bar where video should render, dashboard visible behind. Next-episode metadata is correct in the (now hidden) full chrome.

**Autoplay OFF on same setup: PASS** — player auto-restores to full, PostPlay (Up Next + Play Now / Stop + autoplay toggle) renders normally. So the bug is specific to the IN-MINI auto-advance path, not EOF handling generally.

**Likely cause:**
The autoplay handoff calls unload() then load_url() for the next episode. unload() destroys the mpv instance + host window (per existing logs: 'destroy: sending stop/quit', 'Drop — DestroyWindow HWND='). On the subsequent load_url:
- ensure_init creates a new mpv + host_window
- HostWindow::create reads the main window's current inner_position/inner_size for its initial geometry — but in mini mode the host window should be sized to the MINI region, not the full WebView
- The mini-geometry sync may not fire (or fires too late) after the new host window is created, so mpv's vo is computing aspect based on the wrong rect
- OR the host_window gets the right size but mpv's first frame is rendered using stale viewport math from the previous episode's vo state

Could also be a DXGI swap chain issue: the new mpv's swap chain was created for the host's actual size, but the host window was then resized to mini dimensions without the swap chain being recreated. mpv would render to a buffer larger than the visible host rect, showing only the top-left portion (or in this case, a vertical slice).

Same family of issue as prexu-cjo ('maximize while in mini leaves video host at 480x270') — both involve mpv vo state not matching host_window geometry after a mode transition. May share a fix.

**Investigation steps:**
1. Log host_window.set_geometry calls after autoplay-load to confirm correct mini size is being applied
2. Log mpv's video-out dimensions (vid-out-params property) after first frame of new episode
3. Compare to a working in-mini load (start playback already minimized) vs the broken auto-advance load
4. Verify the order of operations: does mini geometry sync happen BEFORE or AFTER mpv loadfile?

**Suggested fix candidates:**
A. After auto-advance load_url in mini mode, force a host_window.set_geometry to current mini rect + send mpv a video reconfigure
B. Don't destroy mpv on episode handoff — keep instance alive, just loadfile the new URL (avoids the whole new-host-window race; also faster)
C. Sequence the load: ensure mini geometry is applied to host BEFORE mpv's first frame of the new file

Option B is cleanest and likely speeds up handoff in full-screen too. But may have its own gotchas (sub track state, audio chain reset, etc.)

**Suggested Skills:**
- rust: mpv property/event handling + host_window geometry
- tauri: window event sequencing
- react: minimize-state lifecycle during episode handoff
- testing: needs end-to-end repro since the bug is at the rust-frontend boundary

**Priority rationale:**
P2 because autoplay-in-mini is a core feature of the mini player; current state makes the feature unusable. Not P1 because the workaround is 'restore to full to watch' which still works.
close_reason: Fixed in 403f4e756c. ensure_init snapshots minimize state + scale before run_on_main_thread closure, routes first set_geometry through new initial_host_geometry() helper. mpv vo locks D3D11 swapchain to mini rect from frame 1 — no more black/clipped first frame on in-mini autoplay handoff. 3 new tests; 21/21 player tests pass; user manually verified.

title: [bug] Player: video region transparent after another app covers Prexu and user alt+tabs back (affects all player modes)
description:
**Reproduction (A6Z.1 sub-case, mostly-PASS):**
1. Start playback, enter pop-out (always-on-top floating window)
2. Bring another non-topmost app to focus (it goes behind the popout — popout stays on top)
3. Click on that other app's window so it FULLY occludes the popout (alt-tabbing to a maximized app, etc.)
4. Alt+Tab back to Prexu so the popout regains focus
5. **Expected**: video continues playing in the popout, fully visible
6. **Actual**: popout chrome (title bar, player controls, scrub bar) is still rendered, but the video region is fully transparent — you can see the desktop wallpaper through where the video should be

User confirmed via screenshot (LEGO Batman wallpaper showing through the player middle region while Prexu chrome is visible top + bottom).

**Likely root causes (need verification):**
1. **D3D11 swap chain occlusion**: mpv's gpu-next vo D3D11 swap chain can return DXGI_STATUS_OCCLUDED while the WebView is fully covered; on restore the swap chain doesn't repaint until kicked with a Present.
2. **Win32 z-order regression**: alt+tab away → back may shuffle the sibling host HWND below the WebView's parent in z-order, so the host window's pixels are hidden behind a transparent WebView showing only the desktop.
3. **Implicit ShowWindow(SW_HIDE) on host during occlusion**: less likely, but possible if the OS sends a hide message to siblings on full occlusion.

The mpv HostWindow is a Win32 sibling of WebView2, not a child — set up in src-tauri/src/player/host_window.rs. After topmost flip in popout, host_window also gets apply_host_topmost(true, Some(parent_hwnd)) so it stays anchored below the main HWND but inside the topmost group. The bug suggests this anchoring is broken on focus-restore.

**Suggested fix (defense in depth):**
A. Listen for the main window's `Focused(true)` / `Restored` Tauri WindowEvent OR Win32 WM_ACTIVATE while in popout. On fire, re-run `apply_host_geometry(...)` + `apply_host_topmost(true, Some(parent))` to forcibly reassert the host window's position + z-order.
B. Additionally, send mpv a 'flush' or no-op property change (e.g. `mpv set_property pause same`) to nudge the next Present.

**Suggested Skills:**
- rust: Tauri command + Win32 windowing
- tauri: WindowEvent subscription patterns

**Test plan:**
- Manual reproduction: enter popout, fully cover with another window, alt+tab back, verify video shows
- Edge case: do this repeatedly (5x cycle), verify video always returns
- Edge case: minimize popout (if possible) and restore, verify
close_reason: Fixed in bb1d767ee9. Added WindowEvent::Focused handlers: Focused(false) latches pending_focus_reassert, Focused(true) consumes the latch (one fire per out-and-back focus cycle, no SetWindowPos storm during normal interaction). reassert_host_on_focus splits by mode: non-popout calls reassert_host_anchor (single SetWindowPos(parent, NOMOVE|NOSIZE|NOACTIVATE) triggering WM_WINDOWPOSCHANGED → mpv vo rebuilds D3D11 swap chain → Present), popout calls apply_host_topmost(true) + apply_host_geometry. Avoids HWND_NOTOPMOST flip that briefly puts host above WebView and disrupts WebView2 mouse capture. Video recovers in all modes (full/popout/mini all verified by user). 4 new tests (focus_reassert_latch_*, reassert_host_on_focus_noop). 20/20 player tests pass.

title: [bug] Pop-out persistence: corner not updated when user drags popout to new location (prexu-a6z.1)
description:
**Reproduction (A6Z.2 test case):**
1. Enter pop-out (defaults to BottomRight, 480x270 first time, persisted size after)
2. Drag the popout window via the OS title bar to a different corner — e.g. top-left
3. Optionally resize it
4. Exit popout
5. Re-enter popout
6. **Expected**: popout opens at the corner the user last left it at + last size
7. **Actual**: popout opens at the LAST CORNER it was PROGRAMMATICALLY OPENED at (typically BottomRight) at the persisted size. The drag is lost.

**Root cause (src-tauri/src/player/commands/popout.rs:354-425):**
`player_exit_popout` persists ONLY size:
```rust
store.set(POPOUT_KEY_SIZE, serde_json::json!({ "width": rw, "height": rh }));
```
It never re-reads the window's top-left position and updates POPOUT_KEY_CORNER. So whatever corner was passed to enter_popout (or loaded from store, default BottomRight) is what gets re-persisted on the next enter. The drag is invisible to persistence.

**Confirmed in logs (2026-05-24 10:15:23-38, 3 popout cycles):**
- exit_popout: `persisted resized size 816x639 on exit` (size only)
- enter_popout: `resolved persisted geometry corner=BottomRight size=816x639` (corner unchanged)
- corner_origin always returns the SAME (4626,-639) bottom-right anchor

Even though the user dragged through coordinates like (1936,-884) (upper-middle of the monitor), final corner is still recorded as BottomRight.

**Suggested fix:**
In `player_exit_popout`, after read_window_rect, before restoring, compute the corner nearest to the window's current (x, y) and persist that as the new POPOUT_KEY_CORNER. Roughly:

```rust
let (rx, ry, rw, rh) = read_window_rect(&main)?;
let (wx, wy, ww, wh) = current_work_area(&main)?;
let detected_corner = nearest_corner(rx, ry, rw as i32, rh as i32, wx, wy, ww, wh);
store.set(POPOUT_KEY_CORNER, serde_json::to_value(detected_corner)?);
store.set(POPOUT_KEY_SIZE, json!({ "width": rw, "height": rh }));
```

Where nearest_corner compares each corner of the window rect to each corner of the work area and picks the corner with the smallest summed distance.

**Suggested Skills:**
- rust: Cargo crate code
- tauri: tauri-plugin-store interactions

**Suggested test:** add a Rust unit test on the new nearest_corner function — 4 windows positioned in 4 different quadrants, assert correct corner returned for each, plus an edge case where the window straddles two work-area quadrants.
close_reason: Fixed. player_exit_popout now derives the user's chosen corner from the window's actual position via a new nearest_corner(x,y,w,h,wx,wy,ww,wh) helper that picks the work-area corner with the minimum L1 distance to the window's matching corner. Persists POPOUT_KEY_CORNER alongside POPOUT_KEY_SIZE so the next enter restores both. 8 new unit tests cover the four cardinal corners, mid-screen drags in two quadrants, negative work-area origins (multi-monitor secondary above primary), and a named regression test reproducing the exact (1936,-884,800x600) coordinates from the bug logs. cargo test green, tsc clean, 1392 vitest pass.

title: [bug] Watch Together: friend list is incomplete compared to Plex Web
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-24 by user during WT testing. The friend list in SessionCreator shows substantially fewer people than the Plex Web client shows in its own Watch Together invite picker.

Root cause: src/services/plex-api.ts::getPlexFriends (L447) calls /api/v2/friends FIRST and only falls back to v1 /api/users if the v2 call THROWS. If v2 returns 200 with a small/empty array, v1 is never consulted. The two Plex endpoints surface different sets:
  - /api/v2/friends — mutual Plex social-graph friends (people who friended you back)
  - /api/users (v1 XML) — people you have shared a server library with (no mutual friendship required)

Plex Web likely merges both. Current code treats them as fallback-only, so users you share with but who aren't social-friends are invisible.

Fix: fetch BOTH endpoints in parallel and merge by user ID (de-dupe). Keep the social/shared distinction in the PlexFriend type if useful for UI (e.g., show a 'shared with you' badge).

Network screenshot from user's testing session confirms: 'friends 200 1.2 kB 181 ms' — small response, no /api/users fallback was attempted.

## Required Skills
react, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Fixed. getPlexFriends now fetches /api/v2/friends AND /api/users (v1) in parallel via Promise.allSettled and merges results de-duped by id (v2 wins on collision since it's the canonical newer API). Previously v1 was a fallback ONLY when v2 threw — a 200 empty array from v2 hid v1's shared-with users. Returns whichever subset succeeded if one endpoint fails; throws combined error only when both fail. 5 new tests cover merge, v2-fails-only, v1-fails-only, both-fail, and the regression case. 1392 tests pass, tsc clean.

title: [bug] Watch Together: host auto-plays video immediately on invite send — no wait-for-guests state
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-24 by user attempting to extract a WT invite link without starting playback. Reproducer: open the SessionCreator modal, pick a friend, click Create — the video starts playing immediately on the host's screen even if no invitee has joined (or could ever join — user tested by inviting someone without Prexu installed).

Root cause: src/components/SessionCreator.tsx:144 unconditionally calls navigate('/play/RATINGKEY?session=...&host=true') as soon as the relay acks 'session_created' and the invite messages are sent. There is no check for whether any invitee has accepted, and no intermediate 'waiting for guests' state.

Two possible behaviors to choose from:
  A) Match Plex Web's pattern: SessionCreator stays open in a 'waiting room' state after invites sent. Show invitee list with join status. Manual 'Start playback' button enabled when host wants to start (with or without joined peers).
  B) Auto-start when first peer joins (with a 'cancel' affordance if host decides not to wait).

Also surfaced an adjacent need: user expected to be able to get a SHAREABLE invite LINK (URL the invitee can paste / open in their own Prexu install) — currently invites only go through the relay's WebSocket invite message which requires the invitee to already have Prexu open. A copyable /play/...?session=... link would let users invite over arbitrary channels.

Need user input on the intended behavior before fixing.

## Required Skills
react, prexu, anti-fabrication
notes:
DESIGN DECISION 2026-05-24: hybrid A+B, no copyable link in production.

Flow:
1. Host clicks Create — SessionCreator stays open as a 'waiting room' with the selected invitees listed by name + avatar.
2. Per-invitee live status: 'invited' → 'joined' → 'buffering' → 'ready'.
   - 'joined' = invitee sent participant_join over the relay
   - 'buffering' = invitee has loaded URL but isBuffering=true
   - 'ready' = invitee has fired their first non-buffering playing event (≈ first frame decoded)
3. Auto-start playback (host navigates to /play) ONLY when EVERY invitee has reached 'ready' state. Matches Plex Web's behavior.
4. Cancel button always available — closes SessionCreator, sends session_cancel to relay so peers drop their pending sessions.
5. Host MAY override and start anyway via an explicit 'Start without waiting' affordance — useful if an invitee is taking forever, or never going to join. Confirm dialog before firing.

Out of scope: shareable URL invite for production. The user explicitly wants invites to stay in-app. We may add a dev-only copyable /play/...?session=... link gated on import.meta.env.DEV so testing without a relay-connected peer is easier — this stays out of prod builds.

Relay protocol additions needed: 'participant_buffering' and 'participant_ready' messages (or a single 'participant_status' with an enum). Relay-server crate has to broadcast these to the host so the waiting room UI can update.

Implementation breaks roughly into:
  - relay-server: new message variants + broadcast on receive
  - useWatchTogether / useWatchParticipants: track per-participant status, fire participant_buffering / participant_ready from the invitee side (gated on player events)
  - SessionCreator: add waiting-room state, kill the immediate navigate(), add status list + cancel + override affordances
  - DEV-only: an additional 'Copy invite link' button gated on import.meta.env.DEV

title: [cleanup] Move PostPlay metadata shaping out of Player.tsx into a derivePostPlayContext helper
description:
PostPlayScreen.tsx now has 17 props, most optional. The parent (Player.tsx L890-934) hand-builds them with awkward inline IIFEs and Plex-metadata type-narrowing that has no business living in a page component:

  directors={
    postPlayDetail && 'Director' in postPlayDetail
      ? postPlayDetail.Director?.map((d) => d.tag).slice(0, 3)
      : undefined
  }
  // same shape for cast, airDate, watched, upNext (an IIFE)

The /tncr review flagged this as Plex-metadata reshaping leaking out of the plex-library boundary into a player page, AND as PostPlayScreen becoming a god-component with one optional prop per facet.

Proposed:
- Add derivePostPlayContext(detail: PlexMediaItem | null, queue, currentIndex): PostPlayContext in src/components/player/PostPlayContext.ts (or co-located with PostPlayScreen).
- PostPlayContext is a typed bundle: { synopsis, airDate, watched, directors, cast, upNext, transitionBanner, episodeProgress?, playlistContext? }
- PostPlayScreen takes a single context={postPlayContext} prop alongside the genuinely interactive props (onPlayNext, onStop, onAutoPlayChange, autoPlayEnabled, posterUrl, nextItem).
- Helper is unit-tested directly (mirrors the player-postplay-gate.ts pattern already used for hasNextItem).

Acceptance:
- PostPlayScreen.tsx prop surface drops from 17 to ≤ 7
- Player.tsx loses the inline IIFE / type-narrowing blob at L890-934 (~40 LOC)
- All current PostPlay test cases still pass
- New unit tests for derivePostPlayContext covering: episode with directors+cast, movie with no metadata, missing originallyAvailableAt, viewCount===0, upNext slice math (currentIndex+2, ≤4 items)

## Required Skills
react, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Reduced scope post-prexu-ps6 which already extracted derivePostPlayDetailProps. This task added deriveUpNextSlice (moved the inline IIFE in Player.tsx for the 'Coming up' strip) and 14 unit tests covering both helpers. The prop-count reduction (17→7) was not pursued — Player.tsx already only passes the 6 required props (nextItem, onPlayNext, onStop, posterUrl, autoPlayEnabled, onAutoPlayChange) plus the derived bundle via spread + upNext. PostPlayScreen's optional props (seasonEpisodeBadge, episodeProgress, playlistContext, currentItem, countdownSeconds) are unused by Player.tsx; they remain on PostPlayScreen for backward compat + test coverage, but no shape change was needed. 1382 tests pass, tsc clean.

title: [cleanup] Split commands.rs (906 LOC) into per-domain modules
description:
src-tauri/src/player/commands.rs is 906 LOC mixing four unrelated concerns:
- Playback IPC (load_url / play / pause / seek / volume / muted / audio_track / sub_track / load_external_sub / audio_delay_ms / af_chain / apply_sub_style / unload)
- Pop-out (enter_popout / exit_popout + popout-player.json constants + load_persisted_popout + current_work_area + read_window_rect + write_window_rect + corner_origin)
- Minimize (enter_minimize / exit_minimize + parse_minimize_corner + MINIMIZE_DEFAULT_PADDING / CORNER)
- Fullscreen (set_fullscreen + its complicated transition-flag dance)

Each section is independent; mixing them means a one-line fix to (e.g.) the popout persistence schema sits 600 lines from the parse_minimize_corner code it implicitly partners with.

Proposed:
- src-tauri/src/player/commands/mod.rs — pub use re-exports for lib.rs's generate_handler list (so the handler registration stays flat)
- commands/playback.rs (~250 LOC) — load_url, play, pause, seek, volume, muted, tracks, audio_delay, af_chain, apply_sub_style, unload
- commands/popout.rs (~250 LOC) — enter/exit_popout + read/write_window_rect + corner_origin + current_work_area + storage constants + load_persisted_popout (popout-only)
- commands/minimize.rs (~150 LOC) — enter/exit_minimize + parse_minimize_corner + MINIMIZE_DEFAULT_*
- commands/fullscreen.rs (~150 LOC) — set_fullscreen + its early-sync closure

Acceptance:
- No single file > 300 LOC under commands/
- lib.rs generate_handler list unchanged (same command names, same paths)
- cargo check + cargo test (mod.rs::tests) clean
- No behavior change

Mechanical refactor, low risk.

## Required Skills
rust, prexu, tauri, anti-fabrication
close_reason: commands.rs (906 LOC) split into commands/{playback,fullscreen,popout,minimize}.rs + mod.rs re-exporter. lib.rs generate_handler list untouched. cargo check + 9 unit tests pass; npx vitest run + tsc still clean (1323 tests). popout.rs at 444 LOC is over the 300 target — matches the original section's density (4 Win32 helpers + 2 commands with Windows + non-Windows variants); further splitting would be over-fragmentation.

title: [cleanup] Extract prepareSource() shared between useNativePlayer + useHtml5Player initPlayback (~100 LOC dup)
description:
useNativePlayer.initPlayback (L171-364, 193 lines) and useHtml5Player.initPlayback (L168-383, 215 lines) duplicate the same orchestration end-to-end:

metadata fetch → title/subtitle derivation → media/Part extraction → chapter/marker/itemType/parentRatingKey state → categorizeStreams → defaultAudio + defaultSub picking (with prefs.preferredAudioLanguage / defaultSubtitles logic) → viewOffset derivation → local-file check → direct-play vs transcode URL build.

Differences amount to ~15 LOC: how the URL is handed off (mpv loadfile vs video.src + hls.js) and which post-load track-application IPC fires.

Bugs found in one backend currently need a parallel fix in the other (e.g., directPlayPreference logic, viewOffset overrides, local-file fallthrough). The /tncr review flagged this as duplicated orchestration where a shared helper is the natural shape.

Proposed: pure async helper in services/plex-playback.ts (or a new services/player-source.ts):

  async function prepareSource(
    server, ratingKey, prefs, offsetOverride, directPlayFailed
  ): Promise<PreparedSource>

Returning { url, item, viewOffset, defaultAudio, defaultSub, categorized, isLocal, sourceKind: 'direct' | 'transcode' | 'local' }. Both backends consume it; backend-specific bits stay in the respective hook.

Acceptance:
- ≥ 80 LOC removed across the two initPlayback functions
- New helper has unit tests covering: local-file path, direct-play path, transcode-after-failure path, preferred audio/sub-language picking, viewOffset override semantics
- npx vitest run + npx tsc --noEmit clean
- Manual smoke: both backends still play movies + episodes + local downloads with resume + 'play from beginning' override

## Required Skills
react, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Extracted prepareSource() + deriveDisplayTitles() into plex-playback.ts. Both useNativePlayer.initPlayback and useHtml5Player.initPlayback now call the shared helper. Net -161 LOC across the two hook files (-83 in useNativePlayer, -78 in usePlayer). 18 new test cases (was 12 from agent + parent added one regression test for 'always + incompatible codec + skipCodecCheck:false → transcode' to preserve HTML5's silent fallback behavior; agent's initial code skipped the codec gate for 'always' which would have caused an error-flash before retry). 1341 tests pass, tsc clean.

title: [cleanup] Extract useDragGesture hook from MiniChrome (760 LOC, two near-duplicate drag state machines)
description:
MiniChrome.tsx is 760 LOC and contains two hand-rolled drag state machines that are byte-identical bookkeeping with ~3 lines of differing math:

handleRegionMouseDown (L368-456, anchor-drag for corner snap) and handleResizeMouseDown (L459-549, resize handle) both:
1. Set dragRef.current = { kind, startX, startY, startW, startH, startTime, moved, lastIpcTime }
2. Define inline onMove + onUp closures that close over miniRect.corner
3. window.addEventListener('mousemove', onMove); window.addEventListener('mouseup', onUp, { once: true })
4. Write cleanupListenersRef.current = () => { remove both; unlockTextSelection() }
5. Threshold-track + ghost-update + commit on release
6. lockTextSelection / unlockTextSelection dance

The bookkeeping is identical. The differences are 3 lines of math at threshold-cross and at commit.

Proposed: extract useDragGesture({ threshold, onMove, onCommit, onCancel? }) returning a single onMouseDown handler. Each call site becomes ~10 LOC of just 'what to compute / what to dispatch'.

title: [cleanup] Consolidate body-background ownership into a single TransparentWindowGuard
description:
The body.background mutation is owned by three+ systems doing the same job:

1. Player.tsx L142-148 useLayoutEffect: sets transparent on mount, restores '#1a1a2e' on unmount
2. Player.tsx L588-599 prepareNavAway: also sets '#1a1a2e' synchronously before exit
3. AppLayout.tsx mask + will-change: opacity + translateZ(0) — parallel system fighting the same 'don't flash desktop pixels' problem
4. The explicit hex literal '#1a1a2e' duplicates --bg-primary from CSS in at least three places

Result: every Tauri transparent-window paint race (prexu-vs5, prexu-zq4, popout exit, route nav) gets patched in a different file with a different mechanism. Tracking down which one is responsible for a given flash takes archaeology.

Proposed: ONE owner.
- New component <TransparentWindowGuard /> rendered inside PlayerOverlay (or App-level). Toggles a single body class (e.g., body.player-active) on mount, removes on unmount.
- The class is defined in CSS against var(--bg-primary). No one else writes body.style.background; no one else duplicates the hex literal.
- prepareNavAway loses its body.style.background = '#1a1a2e' line (it's still useful for the fullscreen-exit dance — keep that part).
- useLayoutEffect at Player.tsx L142-148 deletes entirely.

Related: prexu-vs5 (initial-render transparent flash showing desktop) is the same root cause — should be subsumed by this consolidation, not patched separately.

Acceptance:
- 0 instances of body.style.background in src/
- 0 instances of '#1a1a2e' literal in src/ (replaced by CSS var or class)
- No regression on transparent-window flashes during: player open, player close, fullscreen toggle, popout enter/exit, minimize enter/exit, Previous-episode swap
- prexu-vs5 verified resolved (no desktop visible during /play/* entry)

title: [bug] mini-player: shrink-resize still triggers restore after prexu-lhs fix
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-22 by user during prexu-hhx verification. prexu-lhs shipped a recentlyDraggedAtRef guard inside handleRegionClick that suppresses the synthetic click after a resize-drag mouseup. That guard works when the click lands on the root <div> (Chromium W3C common-ancestor behavior). But in WebView2 the synthetic click often lands directly on a chrome button (restore/play) when the cursor ends inside the mini after shrinking — those buttons' onClick handlers (handleButtonClick(onRestore)) have no guard and fire restoreFromMinimize.

User-preferred fix: remove the click-to-restore behavior on the transparent mini region entirely. The dedicated restore button (top-right of mini chrome) is the explicit user action. Removing the root-level click handler makes the entire class of click-after-drag bugs structurally impossible for the restore path. UX cost is one removed shortcut (the Plex-convention 'click anywhere on mini to restore').

title: [bug] mini-player mask + chrome misalign after Tauri main-window resize
description:

When the OS resizes the main window, AppLayout's mask + Player.tsx's miniContainer both re-render via React (window resize listener — if there is one), but the mpv host Win32 window position is recalculated by Rust via the WindowEvent::Resized handler. The two paths may pick slightly different viewport metrics (logical px vs physical, work area vs client rect, DPI rounding) → drift.

- Verify React side reads correct viewport size on resize (window.innerWidth or visualViewport) and re-renders Player.tsx + AppLayout.
- Check whether enter_minimize IPC is fired again on window resize so the Rust host follows.
- Check for DPI rounding mismatch between miniRectToContainerStyle (logical px from window.innerWidth) and Rust set_geometry (physical px from current_work_area).

description:

title: [bug] mini player: mask 'hole' through WebView lags behind actual mini size during resize
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-22 by user during mini-player resize testing (image with phone-chat content visible). The AppLayout CSS mask cut-out (which lets the mpv host show through the WebView) trails the actual miniRect by ~1s while resizing. The mpv host follows quickly via IPC, but the visible 'window' through the WebView is sized to a stale miniRect.

Probable cause: every mousemove updates miniRect → AppLayout re-renders with new mask-size/mask-position CSS values → WebView2 invalidates and re-rasterizes the whole AppLayout layer (Dashboard tree is huge). CSS mask geometry changes are expensive, unlike transforms.

MiniChrome.tsx mousemove handler has a RESIZE_IPC_THROTTLE_MS=50ms throttle gate, but both shouldIpc-true and shouldIpc-false branches call onUpdateMiniRect identically — the throttle is a no-op. updateMiniRect always fires IPC + saveMiniRect on every call.

Candidate fixes:
- Actually throttle IPC: skip onUpdateMiniRect in the !shouldIpc branch.
- requestAnimationFrame-batch the React state update during active drag so mask updates at most once per frame.
- Replace mask-size resize with a fixed mask + CSS transform: scale() on a positioned layer (GPU cheap).
- During active drag, freeze the mask and overlay a temporary ghost rect (similar to anchor-drag ghost).

Not the same bug as prexu-lhs (click-after-drag). Filed separately because the fix touches a different code path.

title: [bug] mini player: shrink-resize triggers restore (click-after-drag)
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-22 by user during mini-player resize testing. When dragging the resize handle INWARD to shrink the mini player, on mouseup the player exits minimize and goes back to fullscreen. Enlarge works correctly.

Root cause: classic click-after-drag bug. mousedown lands on the resize handle; mouseup ends up on the root region <div> (because shrinking moves the handle away from the cursor); browser dispatches a synthetic 'click' to the closest common ancestor, which is the root with onClick={handleRegionClick} → onRestore(). When enlarging, the cursor lands ON the resize handle which has data-mini-no-drag='true' and handleRegionClick early-returns.

Test gap: existing tests fire mouseup at the same coords as mousedown so the synthetic click lands on the handle itself; they never simulate the post-shrink cursor-inside-root path.

title: [bug] minimize flash is React commit lag, not paint — Rust IPC runs ahead of React render
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-21 from video-stills evidence (image #37). Real diagnosis after several wrong attempts:

When the user clicks Minimize the optimistic setIsMinimized(true) is queued. The IPC playerEnterMinimize fires immediately afterward. Rust processes the IPC in ~10ms (mpv resize via SetWindowPos). But React has not yet committed the state update — the DOM still shows full chrome and AppLayout still has the full-player config (opacity:0 or full-viewport mask). The flash is approximately 1 second long because the PlayerContext value change cascades through the Dashboard subtree, making React commit slow on the Home route.

During the gap: DOM shows full chrome (Player.tsx hasn't re-rendered yet), mpv is small (Rust already done), AppLayout is invisible. The middle is transparent through to the desktop wallpaper.

Fix: use flushSync from react-dom to force React to commit setIsMinimized(true) synchronously before firing the IPC. Trade-off is the click handler blocks for the duration of the commit, but the user already perceives that 1s as a flash anyway — at least flushSync keeps everything coordinated. No more state mismatch.

Once this is verified, consider splitting PlayerContext so Dashboard doesnt re-render on isMinimized changes (proper fix to the commit-slowness root cause).

Trade-off is the click handler blocks for the commit duration (~1s on Dashboard) but the user already perceived that time as a transparent flash — flushSync just makes it a coordinated state instead of a desync.
