title: [bug] restoreFromMinimize optimistic update causes transparent flash while Rust resizes mpv back to full
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-21 after DOM-verified user testing. Correcting earlier misdiagnosis of prexu-cay/prexu-9bk.

Real cause: PlayerContext.restoreFromMinimize optimistically flips isMinimized to false BEFORE the IPC to Rust. React then immediately swaps:
- AppLayout: corner mask → full-viewport mask (AppLayout becomes entirely invisible)
- Player.tsx: mini wrapper → full chrome

BUT Rust still has mpv at the small minimize-size. While Rust resizes mpv back to full (SetWindowPos, several hundred ms on multi-monitor 4K setups), AppLayout is invisible everywhere and mpv only covers the small corner. The rest of the WebView is transparent through to the Tauri main window (transparent) and out to the desktop wallpaper. Observed as the 1s transparent flash the user reports.

Image #32 (DOM-confirmed): full chrome rendered + small mpv preview at corner + desktop visible elsewhere = the exact state mismatch.

Fix: drop the optimistic update in restoreFromMinimize. Wait for IPC to resolve before setIsMinimized(false). The brief lag (mpv resizes while React still shows mini chrome) is harmless because AppLayout's corner mask covers everything outside the corner; mpv expands behind AppLayout's opaque content. When React then flips to full-player and AppLayout becomes invisible, mpv is already full size and visible everywhere.

KEEP the optimistic update on minimize — that direction is actually safe because AppLayout switches from full-viewport mask to corner mask immediately; the corner hole exposes a slice of the still-full mpv frame (visible content) until Rust shrinks it, no desktop transparency.

title: [bug] opacity:0 still skipped paint on Dashboard, ~1s flash persists (residual after prexu-cay)
description:

WebView2 (and Chromium in general) is permitted to skip painting opacity:0 subtrees as an optimization. When opacity flips back to 1, paint must run from scratch — on a heavy Dashboard (HeroSlideshow + 3 poster rows + sidebar) that's the ~1s observed.

AppLayout stays at opacity:1 always. The layer is continuously painted; mask compositing decides what's visible. Mask-size and mask-position changes are GPU compositor operations — no paint pass required on transition.

Keep pointerEvents:none for isFullPlayer so clicks pass through to PlayerOverlay.

title: [bug] AppLayout visibility:hidden causes paint-on-reveal delay when entering minimize (residual after prexu-di9)
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-21 after prexu-di9 optimistic-update fix landed. The IPC-side lag is gone, but the user can still briefly see through to the desktop on Dashboard route when entering minimize.

Root cause: AppLayout uses visibility:hidden during full-player mode (visibility:visible only when minimize is active). When visibility flips, the browser has to repaint AppLayout's children from scratch — paint was SKIPPED during the hidden window because visibility:hidden tells the browser not to bother. Dashboard has HeroSlideshow + Continue Watching + Recently Added rows + sidebar; that first paint after the visibility flip takes ~50–150ms, during which the user sees through to the desktop wallpaper.

Fix: switch the hide mechanism from visibility:hidden to opacity:0. Opacity is GPU-composited — browser paints AppLayout into its compositor layer normally; rendering at zero alpha just composites with 0 alpha. The layer stays warm the entire player session, so when opacity flips back to 1 it's a compositor toggle (instant) instead of a paint pass (delayed).

The prexu-kfa concern about display:none breaking VirtualizedLibraryGrid's clientWidth/ResizeObserver doesn't apply to opacity:0 — opacity doesn't affect layout, dimensions, or observers.

Keep pointerEvents:none so clicks still pass through to PlayerOverlay during full-player. Keep visibility:visible (the default) since we're using opacity now.

title: [bug] popout enter/exit cycle grows main window outer size each round trip
description:
Reported during prexu-j18 testing (2026-05-20). User started with the main window entirely on one monitor at a normal size. After one enter_popout + exit_popout cycle, the restored window was (1595,-2160,1934x2167) — 2167px tall, larger than a 2160px monitor.

Likely cause: enter_popout captures main.outer_position() / main.outer_size() via Tauri's API, which on Windows 11 may include the invisible DWM border / extended frame bounds. exit_popout then calls set_size with that captured value, which Win32 treats as the OUTER rect — but the OS recomputes the actual visible chrome, resulting in a slightly larger window. Each cycle accumulates the discrepancy.

Known analog: GetWindowRect (Tauri outer_size) vs DwmGetWindowAttribute(DWMWA_EXTENDED_FRAME_BOUNDS) (what visually represents the window). set_size and outer_size use different definitions.

Fix options:
- Use inner_size on stash (smaller, but accurate to what user 'sees') and restore via inner-to-outer adjustment.
- Query DwmGetWindowAttribute(DWMWA_EXTENDED_FRAME_BOUNDS) for both stash and apply, normalizing the measurement.
- Snapshot the Tauri 'logical' size via WebviewWindow::scale_factor() math and re-apply via the same path.

Reproducer: enter popout from a normal-sized window, drag corner once or not at all, exit popout. Compare outer_size before vs after.

## Required Skills
rust, tauri, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Fixed in 09f3e8f. enter_popout now stashes outer rect via Win32 GetWindowRect; exit_popout restores via Win32 SetWindowPos. Same Win32 coord system both ways = exact round-trip, no DWM-invisible-border drift, no per-cycle growth. cargo check clean.

title: [bug] sync_geometry consumes stale pending_geometry, host stuck at old size
description:
Existing prexu-cjo bug, exact reproducer now captured (2026-05-20):
- User double-clicks title bar to maximize, then double-clicks again to restore.
- Logs show: sync_geometry(maximize) gets throttled+stored as pending. 2 seconds later sync_geometry(restore) arrives and is non-throttled. The non-throttled branch does pending.take().unwrap_or(current_args), which prefers the STALE pending (maximize) over the FRESH current args (restore).
- Result: mpv host stuck at maximize geometry (3840x2137) while main window is restored (1934x2167). Visible seam where host extends beyond WebView.

Fix: in sync_geometry's non-throttled path, always apply the CURRENT call args. Clear pending (discard it). Rationale: every Resized event triggers a fresh sync_geometry, so the latest call already has the freshest data; pending exists only as a 'last drag position never lost' safety net, but the next event already covers that case. Worst case after fix: a drag that ends within the 50ms throttle window may miss the final ~50ms of motion, which is imperceptible. Likely supersedes prexu-cjo.

## Required Skills
rust, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Fixed in fb672ba. apply_host_topmost now re-anchors below parent on the topmost=false path; sync_geometry now always applies current call args and discards stale pending. cargo check clean. User to verify in dev build.

title: [bug] mini-player: video host not topmost, other apps cover the video
description: From 2026-05-09 testing (correction to prexu-ltu issue #2): when mini-player is active, the Tauri WebView overlay floats above other apps thanks to set_always_on_top(true) on the main window, BUT the mpv video host stays in the regular z-order so other apps render BETWEEN the always-on-top overlay and the video underneath. User saw the player chrome / time / next-episode controls floating above their screenshot tool, but the actual video was hidden behind it. Root cause: HostWindow is a WS_POPUP sibling top-level window (host_window.rs:7); set_always_on_top on the Tauri WebViewWindow only flips the topmost flag for that HWND, not the sibling host. Fix: added HostWindow::set_topmost(bool) using SetWindowPos with HWND_TOPMOST/HWND_NOTOPMOST + a PlayerState::apply_host_topmost wrapper that also re-anchors below the main window in the topmost group. enter_mini calls apply_host_topmost(true) after the main set_always_on_top + geometry; exit_mini calls apply_host_topmost(false). Now both windows are topmost together with the WebView overlaying the video, and other apps cannot cover either one.
close_reason: Added HostWindow::set_topmost(bool) using SetWindowPos with HWND_TOPMOST/HWND_NOTOPMOST + PlayerState::apply_host_topmost(topmost, parent) accessor that re-anchors the host below the main window inside the topmost group. enter_mini now calls apply_host_topmost(true, Some(main_hwnd)) after geometry sync; exit_mini calls apply_host_topmost(false, None). Both the WebView overlay AND the mpv video are now topmost together (with the WebView still anchored above the host in z-order), so other apps cannot render between them. cargo check clean.

title: [bug] alphabet scrubber lands short and below target row
description: Two related symptoms when clicking a letter higher in the alphabet (e.g. 'D' from a viewport showing 'A-C'): (1) the first click scrolls but doesn't reach the target letter's first row — that row is below the viewport. A second click scrolls further but only just barely shows the target row at the bottom. (2) The user expects the target row at the TOP of the viewport, not the bottom. Root cause: VirtualizedLibraryGrid's scrollToIndex was calling tanstack/react-virtual's virtualizer.scrollToIndex with align:'center' + behavior:'smooth'. For long alphabet jumps the virtualizer estimates row sizes for unmeasured rows; cumulative estimate error puts the smooth scroll at the wrong offset. Once the destination window renders, measureElement updates the cache but the smooth scroll has already settled. Fix: changed to align:'start', behavior:'auto', and added two RAF settle passes that re-issue scrollToIndex once the just-rendered rows have been measured. Surfaced 2026-05-09 by Nick during library testing on a ~500-show library.
close_reason: Fixed in src/components/VirtualizedLibraryGrid.tsx scrollToIndex: switched align:'center' → 'start' (target row at top of viewport, matching user expectation for alphabet jumps) and behavior:'smooth' → 'auto' (instant). Added a two-RAF settle loop that re-issues scrollToIndex on the next animation frame and again the frame after — by then the just-revealed rows have been measured by the virtualizer's measureElement callback, so the corrected (rather than estimated) cumulative offset gets applied. Non-virtualized fallback also switched to block:'start' for consistency. tsc + vitest 1232/1232 clean. Browser verification still required (the bug only reproduces on a virtualized library with hundreds of rows + a long alphabet jump).

title: Player as overlay (not route) — instant exit + page persistence
description:
**Goal**: clicking Stop returns the user to the page they were on with **no perceptible gap** — matching Plex's UX. Today, Player is a peer route to AppLayout (App.tsx:200-219), so stopping triggers a full AppLayout + destination route remount (~1s in dev, prexu-zq4). The fix is architectural: keep AppLayout always mounted, render Player as a top-level overlay driven by context, never unmount the underlying page when entering playback.

User-confirmed scope (2026-05-04):
- OK to lose: URL-based deep-linking into /play/X, browser back/forward toggling player open/closed, bookmarks of /play/X.
- Must keep: in-player Prev/Next episode for shows + playlists (currently navigate(`/play/${next}`)).
- Defer: Watch Together URL-based entry (separate WT refactor later — single-user UX is the priority).

## Architecture

**New: src/contexts/PlayerContext.tsx**
- State: `{ playing: { ratingKey: string; offset?: number; session?: WTOptions } | null }`
- Methods: `play(ratingKey, options?)`, `stop()`, `replaceRatingKey(nextKey)` (for in-player prev/next without unmount/remount churn)
- Provider mounted at App.tsx top level under AppProviders

**Modified: src/App.tsx**

title: Stop click: navy exit overlay visible for ~2s before navigate
description:
Symptom (2026-05-03 user report on dev tauri build): clicking Stop on the native player paints the navy exitOverlay (introduced by prexu-cuq) and leaves it visible for about 2 seconds before the previous page renders.

Root cause: src-tauri/src/player/mod.rs destroy() did a synchronous bounded-join on the mpv event pump with a 2s timeout (recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(2))). The TS handleExit awaits player_unload, so when the join hits its timeout (often, in practice — mpv's wait_event(1.0) only breaks on the NEXT iteration after Shutdown), the React UI is held up for the full 2s. The exit overlay sits on screen for that whole window.

Fix: split destroy() into a synchronous silence phase (mute, pause, queue stop, queue quit) and a background teardown phase (pump join, host drop, terminate_destroy on Inner drop). The Tauri command returns in <50ms after silence; the slow teardown happens on a spawned thread without blocking the user. Audio is already cut by the synchronous mute, so the deferred work has no user-visible cost.

## Required Skills
rust,tauri,prexu,anti-fabrication
close_reason: destroy() now returns synchronously after mute+pause+stop+quit; pump join + host drop + terminate moved to a background thread so handleExit's await resolves in <50ms instead of up to 2s.

title: Next Episode prompt fires on movies due to stale queue + missing content-type gate
description:
**Symptom (verified)**: Played 10 Cloverfield Lane (a movie). Near end credits, a 'Next Episode' button appeared. Clicked it — went to Yu Yu Hakusho S01E02, which had been the test content from a prior day's session.

**Root cause** (found): src/pages/Player.tsx:335-337:
```ts
const hasNextItem =
  queue.currentIndex + 1 < queue.items.length ||
  episodeNav.handleNextEpisode != null;
```

For a movie, episodeNav.handleNextEpisode is null (correct — Plex movies don't have episode siblings). But queue.items still contains yesterday's Yu Yu Hakusho episode because src/contexts/QueueContext.tsx:48 persists the queue to localStorage and never clears it. So hasNextItem becomes true via the queue-fallback branch, useSkipSegments synthesizes the 'Next Episode' segment (commit 8e42202), and clicking it advances the queue to whatever's there.

**Two distinct issues, one user impact**:

1. **Synthesis doesn't gate by content type.** A 'Next Episode' label is meaningless for a movie. Even with a populated queue, clicking it shouldn't say 'Next Episode' for a movie. Either:
   - Gate hasNextItem synthesis to player.itemType === 'episode' (current item is an episode, so 'next' makes sense), OR
   - Rename the synthesized prompt to 'Up Next' / 'Play Next' for non-episode contexts, OR
   - Don't synthesize at all when current item isn't an episode.

2. **Queue persists stale items across app sessions.** Even if (1) is fixed for movies, a queued YYH ep from yesterday surviving into today is questionable UX. Options:
   - Clear queue on app launch (aggressive — loses 'oh I queued these last night, let me resume').
   - Add per-item timestamps + TTL (e.g. 24h).
   - Clear queue on Stop / when leaving the player without auto-advancing.
   - Leave as-is and rely on (1) to gate the prompt — accept that the queue may contain unrelated content but only surface it when the *current* content matches its type.

Recommend doing (1) first — minimal risk, fixes the user-visible bug directly. Decide on (2) afterward as separate UX policy.

**Acceptance**: While playing a movie, no 'Next Episode' button appears, even with non-empty queue. While playing an episode with a queued unrelated item (e.g. movie), behavior is intentional per the (2) decision.

## Required Skills
react,prexu
notes: Reproduced 2026-05-03 by user after testing prexu-aua. ratingKey for 10 Cloverfield Lane was 26800; clicking Next Episode took user to YYH S01E02. Queue persistence file: src/contexts/QueueContext.tsx:48.
close_reason:
Fixed in src/pages/Player.tsx by introducing a single 'logical next' notion (hasNextItem = itemType==='episode' AND (queue has next OR Plex episode-nav has next)) and threading it to all three sites that previously had per-call-site gates that didn't agree:
1. useSkipSegments hasNextEpisode arg — was already gated indirectly via hasNextItem prior to this change.
2. SkipSegmentButton hasNextEpisode prop — was '!!handleNextEpisode' (always true). Now uses hasNextItem.
3. PostPlayScreen useEffect trigger — was 'remainingCount > 0' (any queue items). Now uses hasNextItem.

Verified by user on dev: movies show 'Skip Credits' (not 'Next Episode') and don't pop PostPlay at end. Episodes still get 'Next Episode' + PostPlayScreen at credits/EOF (regression-free).

title: Audio leaks 2-3s after Stop — destroy is async to React unmount
description:
**Symptom**: Clicking Stop in the player. Video disappears immediately, dashboard renders (background painted), but audio continues playing for ~2-3 seconds before fully cutting out.

**Reproduced** on installed Prexu_0.6.3 (commit 09054f0) playing ratingKey=63168 with audio enhancements preset=night. Logs show full destroy() sequence completes within the same wall-clock second (mute → pause → stop → quit → pump joined in 18ms → host dropped at 28ms total). So the leak isn't from a slow Rust-side teardown.

**Root cause**: src/hooks/player/useNativePlayer.ts:382 fires `invoke("player_unload")` from a useEffect cleanup. useEffect cleanups CANNOT be async — returning a Promise from one is a no-op for React. So when the player unmounts:
1. React cleanup runs, fires `invoke("player_unload")` and immediately returns
2. React continues unmounting the player overlay → navigates back → dashboard renders → user sees background paint
3. *Concurrently*, Tauri command thread is still inside Player::destroy() doing mute/pause/stop/quit/join/mpv_terminate_destroy
4. mpv's audio-buffer (~200ms decoded-ahead) + WASAPI's already-submitted samples continue playing during steps 2-3

The historical 'fix' captured in the superseded memory ('destroy() joins event pump before dropping Inner so mpv_terminate_destroy runs sync and silences audio') is correct on the Rust side but doesn't help — the Rust side runs concurrently with React's unmount, not before it.

**Fix candidates** (not decided):
1. **React side**: Move player_unload OUT of useEffect cleanup. Have the Stop button (and PostPlayScreen Stop, and back-nav) AWAIT `invoke("player_unload")` before navigating. Component then unmounts after mpv is already terminated. Simplest fix; won't catch unmount-via-tab-close paths.
2. **Rust side**: Detach WASAPI before stop. Set `ao=null` (or `command ["ao_reload"]`) at the start of destroy() to immediately route mpv audio to a null sink, killing the OS audio session within mpv's tick rather than waiting for buffered samples to drain. Keeps the React side simple but adds a libmpv2 audio API touchpoint.
3. **Both**: 1 for the fast path, 2 as backstop for unmount-via-other-paths.

Recommend evaluating fix 1 first — small, contained to useNativePlayer.ts, and explains the user-observed race directly.

**Acceptance**: After Stop, audio cuts within 200ms of video disappearing (or simultaneously). Reproduce by playing 30s into any file then clicking Stop.

## Required Skills
react,rust,prexu,anti-fabrication
notes: Surfaced during prexu-2zo.3 retest 2026-05-03. Smoke test still soft-passing on direct play but this is a clear UX regression vs the SUPERSEDED 'fullscreen-host-window-geometry-sync-confirmed' memory which claimed destroy was synchronous end-to-end.
close_reason: Fixed in src/pages/Player.tsx handleExit: await invoke('player_unload') BEFORE navigate(target). The useEffect cleanup that ran after unmount was firing player_unload fire-and-forget, racing mpv terminate against React's next paint and leaving 2-3s of buffered audio playing while the dashboard was already showing. Awaiting in handleExit pushes the route change until destroy() returns; the cleanup then no-ops via 'destroy: nothing to destroy'. User-verified: 'audio is correctly cut off on stop'.

title: Native player: 30s first-play cold start on installed build
description:
**Symptom**: First HEVC playback after a fresh install takes ~30 seconds before first frame is presented. Subsequent plays (after closing/reopening Prexu) start in <1 second. Reproduced on installed Prexu_0.6.3_x64-setup.exe at C:\Users\grand\AppData\Local\Prexu\prexu.exe.

**Diagnostic data** (from %LOCALAPPDATA%\com.prexu.client\logs\Prexu.log, UTC):

First play (cold):
```
23:19:01  load_url offset=94339ms
23:19:01  ensure_init OK, HostWindow created, loadfile sent
   ⏱ 12 second gap with NO log entries from mpv
23:19:13  apply_sub_style fires (React side reacted to something — likely FileLoaded that we didn't observe in this session at our log level)
```

Second play of a different file (warm), after closing/reopening Prexu:
```
23:26:27  load_url offset=120500ms
23:26:27  ensure_init OK, HostWindow created, mpv created, loadfile sent
23:26:27  FileLoaded → player://ready    ← all in same wall-clock second
```

**Likely contributors** (from highest to lowest confidence):
1. Windows Defender / AV first-execution scan of the unsigned Prexu.exe (19MB) and libmpv-2.dll (117MB). Plausible because second run is unaffected.
2. Cold mpv hwdec probing (auto-safe → d3d11va selection on Windows).
3. Cold Plex direct-streaming connection — first byte-range request to plex.direct on a fresh run can stall.

**Possible mitigations to evaluate** (no decision yet):
- Code-sign the installer/binary (prevents some AV scan delays; needs signing cert).
- Warm-init mpv at app launch (create a dummy player + libmpv handle so the DLL is paged in and hwdec is probed before user clicks play). Trade-off: small idle CPU/memory cost.
- 'Preparing playback…' UI affordance during the cold-load phase so the user knows we're working.
- Investigate whether the 12s gap is mpv side or Plex side by adding more granular logging (track when first byte-range response arrives).

**Acceptance**: First-play time-to-first-frame on a fresh install drops to ≤5s OR an unambiguous loading affordance is shown during the cold load. Whichever lands first.

title: Episode list: hover thumbnail to play, click text to view detail
description:
On the season episode list (e.g. /item/<show>/season/<n> — see screenshot of Yu Yu Hakusho 'Spirit Detective Saga' S1), each episode row has a thumbnail on the left and metadata (episode #, date, title, description) on the right. Currently the entire row is one click target that goes to the episode detail page.

Proposed:
- **Hover the THUMBNAIL** → reveals a play overlay (similar to dashboard cards). Clicking the thumbnail starts playback directly via /play/<episodeKey>.
- **Click the right-side text region** (episode #, date, title, description) → unchanged behaviour: navigates to the episode detail page (/item/<episodeKey>).

This makes binge-watching dramatically faster — no detail page round-trip — while keeping the existing per-episode info path for users who want to read details first.

Considerations:
- Hover-to-play on TV/remote doesn't translate; keyboard focus on the thumbnail should also reveal the play affordance, and Enter on a focused thumbnail starts playback.
- For mobile/touch: tap on thumbnail = play, tap on text = detail (no hover needed).
- Should respect viewOffset (resume vs from-start) — same logic as the existing play buttons on detail pages.

Acceptance:
- Hovering an episode's thumbnail on desktop reveals a play button overlay.
- Clicking the play overlay (or the thumbnail) navigates to /play/<ratingKey> with the saved viewOffset.
- Clicking anywhere in the right-side text column navigates to /item/<ratingKey> as today.
- Keyboard focus on the thumbnail also shows the play affordance and Enter triggers playback.

title: Re-enable autoplay UX flow after Stop/disable
description:
Once a user clicks 'Stop' on PostPlayScreen or unchecks the AUTO PLAY ON checkbox, there's no UI surface to re-enable autoplay for subsequent episodes' PostPlay overlays. autoplayEnabled is local React state in PostPlayScreen, so it resets to true when the overlay re-mounts — but if EOF detection breaks (or the user navigates via SkipSegmentButton's Next Episode instead of letting EOF fire), they may go many episodes without seeing the PostPlay overlay at all and lose track of the setting.

Surfaced during Phase 3 dev verification: user lost the autoplay flow entirely once SkipSegmentButton-driven navigation took over.

Possible fixes:
- Persist autoplay-on preference in playback prefs (alongside skipIntroEnabled / skipCreditsEnabled).
- Add a small autoplay toggle in PlayerControls or in a settings flyout so it's always reachable.
- Show the PostPlay overlay's autoplay toggle even when the next-episode jump came from SkipSegmentButton (would require triggering PostPlay on user-initiated nav, which is a different UX).

title: Add Stop button in player controls; make Back navigate history
description:
The current back-arrow in PlayerControls performs two roles:
- Exit the player (return to library)
- Implicitly halt playback (player_unload runs in cleanup)

After clicking Next Episode (or letting autoplay advance), clicking Back returns to the PREVIOUS episode (browser history pop) instead of exiting the player. From the user's perspective there's no obvious way to stop watching short of using ESC or pressing Back multiple times.

Proposal:
- Replace (or augment) the back arrow with an explicit Stop button that quits the player session entirely (navigate to /library or to the last non-/play/* route).
- Or keep both: Back = navigate(-1) (history pop), Stop = navigate to safe non-player route.
- Stop should also dispatch player_unload eagerly so audio stops immediately even if the next route's render is slow.

Discussion needed on:
- Which icon / position
- Whether to map Stop to a keyboard shortcut (Q? Shift+Esc?)
- TV-app future: a remote 'Back' button typically maps to history-pop; we need a separate 'Exit' or 'Home' affordance for the player

Could be combined with prexu-w0j (Skip Credits / Next Episode separation) since both are player-control UX rethinks.

Surfaced during Phase 3 dev verification.

title: ErrorBoundary: Cannot read properties of null (reading '1') during HMR
description:
During an active dev session with Vite HMR firing on backend pushes, the React ErrorBoundary fallback briefly displayed 'Something went wrong / Cannot read properties of null (reading '1')'. Playback resumed afterward. Likely a transient HMR state where one module reloaded before another caught up.

Reproducer attempt: difficult — only seen during heavy edit-save cycles in dev. Production builds shouldn't hit this since modules aren't replaced live.

Triage path:
1. Check src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx — capture err.stack and componentStack to a logger so we have a stack trace next time. console.error already exists but doesn't surface in our log file.
2. Grep for any `x[1]` / `x['1']` patterns where x might be null after a dev-time reload.
3. Common HMR offenders: useRef holding stale refs after module replacement, useMemo/useCallback retaining old closures, event listeners attached to torn-down DOM nodes.
4. If reproducible, capture the browser DevTools stack trace (Vite shows mapped sources) — that's the fastest path to root cause.

Surfaced during Phase 3 dev verification.

## Required Skills
react,prexu
close_reason: Instrumented ErrorBoundary.componentDidCatch to log error.name/message/stack and componentStack via logger.error so the next HMR trip leaves a trace in the tauri log file. Phase B grep was clean (no credible arr[1] null-read in our code paths); the runtime '[1]' is most likely inside a third-party dep during module replacement, which the new logger output will pin down on next reproduction. Test added covering logger call. vitest 1188/1188, tsc clean.

title: Skip segment dismiss persists across episodes
description:
When the user clicks the 'x' to dismiss the SkipSegmentButton (intro/credits/Next Episode prompt), the dismissal silently persists into subsequent episodes — the button never reappears.

Two compounding causes in useSkipSegments.ts:
1. useSegments cache key is markers.length + chapters.length only (line 106-110). Most TV episodes have 2 markers (intro + credits), so the cache returns the OLD episode's segment objects with their old startMs values. The dismissedRef set keys on `${type}-${startMs}` and matches the stale entries — segments get filtered out before reaching state.
2. dismissedRef (line 37) is a per-hook-instance useRef with no reset. Player.tsx stays mounted across React Router navigations, so the Set lives forever for the session.

Fix: switch useSegments cache to reference equality on markers/chapters arrays (Plex returns fresh arrays per metadata fetch, so === changes between episodes). Reset dismissedRef in a useEffect when markers/chapters references change.

## Required Skills
react,prexu
close_reason: Fixed in commit ff149b3. useSegments cache now keys on markers/chapters reference identity (was length-only, which collided across same-shape episodes). Added a useEffect in useSkipSegments that clears dismissedRef + activeSegment when markers or chapters references change — Player.tsx stays mounted across React Router navigations so the Set otherwise persisted for the entire session. Existing 9 useSkipSegments tests pass; full vitest suite 1163/1163 pass.

title: PostPlay: Stop button doesn't stop playback when autoplay is still on
description:
Clicking Stop on PostPlayScreen while 'AUTO PLAY ON' is checked closes the overlay but playback restarts (the next episode keeps loading/playing). Workaround: uncheck AUTO PLAY ON first, then click Stop — that actually halts.

Likely cause: handlePostPlayStop in Player.tsx:243 only does setShowPostPlay(false). It doesn't:
- Cancel the PostPlayScreen autoplay-on-zero countdown interval
- Pause/unload the player

So the autoplay countdown keeps ticking even after the overlay is hidden, eventually firing onPlayNext via the unmount cleanup or interval racing the unmount. Or a second EOF-reaction handler runs because the underlying player wasn't paused.

Likely fix:
- onStop in PostPlayScreen should ALSO clear the autoplay interval (mirror handleToggleAutoPlay's clearInterval branch).
- handlePostPlayStop should also explicitly pause the player or call player_unload to stop the underlying playback (which also resolves bug #1's underlying-playback-progressing concern).

Acceptance: with AUTO PLAY ON checked, clicking Stop closes the overlay AND halts playback (no further loading, no audio bleed-through). Same behavior as clicking Stop with autoplay off.

Related to prexu-postplay-autoplay (filed alongside) — probably fixable in one change.

## Required Skills

close_reason: Root cause was the event pump emitting player://eof for every libmpv EndFile event regardless of reason. STOP/QUIT (which fire when player_unload tears down mpv during episode-change navigation) were misinterpreted as natural EOF, so when the user clicked the SkipSegmentButton's 'Next Episode' label (mid-credits), the OLD player's STOP fired player://eof, React showed PostPlay for the queue position AFTER the just-loaded next ep, and the audio/video underneath was the legitimately-loaded new episode. Fixed in events.rs by filtering EndFile to reason==0 (EOF only). Player.tsx also got defensive player_pause dispatches on PostPlay open and Stop click as belt-and-suspenders for any future regression. cargo check + tsc clean.

title: Tighten release.yml cleanup tag pattern
description:
The dev-drprasad/delete-older-releases step in release.yml uses delete_tag_pattern: 'v' which is a regex that matches any tag containing 'v'. After 6.1, the recommended setup involves a 'libmpv-vendor' release tag — the cleanup would delete that vendored archive after enough version bumps, breaking the next CI run.

Fix: change pattern to ^v\d+\.\d+\.\d+$ (strict semver vN.N.N).

Acceptance: tag a fake release like 'libmpv-vendor-2026' and run the cleanup logic locally / verify it isn't selected for deletion.
close_reason: Tightened delete_tag_pattern in .github/workflows/release.yml from 'v' (regex matching any tag containing v, including libmpv-vendor) to '^v\d+\.\d+\.\d+$' (strict semver vN.N.N). Used single-quoted YAML scalar so the backslashes are literal regex metacharacters (in double-quoted YAML, \d would be an invalid escape). Won't auto-clean pre-release tags like v0.6.3-rc1 either, which is fine — those tend to want longer retention for triage. Will be exercised on next v* release tag push.

title: Native player: continue-watching prompt is slow to appear
description:
Clicking play on a partially-watched item shows blank for 1-2 seconds before the 'Continue from xx:xx / Start from beginning' prompt appears. Caught during Phase 2 acceptance.

Likely causes:
- getItemMetadata Plex API call latency (network-bound; hard to fix without caching)
- useNativePlayer's setIsLoading sequence holds back the prompt component until metadata resolves
- The prompt is a separate hook/component that waits on player metadata that didn't have to wait before

Investigation: profile the path between play-click and prompt-render. Add timing logs.

Acceptance: prompt appears within ~200ms of click for items with cached metadata, or shows a loading indicator immediately if data is still being fetched.

## Required Skills
react,prexu
close_reason: Resolved on feature/native-player. 3uj: mpv default osd-level=1/osd-bar=yes drew a horizontal progress bar in the middle of the video on every seek; with the transparent webview it showed through alongside our React seek bar. Set osd-level=0 and osd-bar=no on mpv init. dcj: usePlayAction now trusts cached item.viewOffset (always present on onDeck/recents) and shows ResumePopover instantly with no network round trip. Slow path (no cached offset) shows a small loading popover at click point for immediate feedback.

title: Step 6.4 — code-signing impact assessment
description:
libmpv-2.dll is third-party — does Windows SmartScreen flag it inside the bundle? Does existing code-signing path cover bundled resources or do we need to also sign the DLL? Document findings.

## Required Skills
github,security,documentation
close_reason: Documented findings in docs/code-signing.md. Key conclusions: (1) No Authenticode signing exists today — TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY is for the updater (minisign), not signtool. (2) SmartScreen does not inspect bundled DLLs; warnings are caused by the unsigned installer/.exe themselves and would exist with or without libmpv. (3) Once we add Authenticode signing, also sign libmpv-2.dll (in CI before tauri-action runs, so the build.rs copy carries the signature into the bundle) — large unsigned third-party DLLs occasionally trip AV heuristics. (4) Recommended workflow change documented (signCommand in tauri.conf.json, EV cert via thumbprint, mandatory /tr timestamp). (5) Code signing is separate tech debt — not blocking the Phase 6 ship.

title: Path C: DComp visual hosting (fork wry + mpv render API)
description:
The 'proper' single-surface fix if Path A fails. Convert the webview to ICoreWebView2CompositionController so mpv's swapchain visual and the webview visual live in one DirectComposition tree on the main window with true z-order + transparency. 

SCOPE (large): (1) fork wry or build a custom WebView2 host using ICoreWebView2CompositionController + RootVisualTarget; re-implement input forwarding, focus, IME, and DPI/rasterization-scale handling that windowed mode does for free. (2) Replace mpv --wid embedding with the libmpv render API: OpenGL via ANGLE + WGL_NV_DX_interop2 to share the GL color texture into a D3D11 texture, presented on a DComp visual (keeps hwdec). libmpv2 4.1.0 RenderContext covers the OpenGL path; software path would need raw libmpv2_sys FFI and defeats hwdec.

No Tauri/wry Windows precedent (demonstrated only on macOS). Highest risk/effort. Only pursue if Path A's sibling-airspace proves unworkable.

title: Auto re-discover server connection when stored URL unreachable
description:
## Why
User changed router; Plex server got new DHCP IP. App restores stored server URL blindly at boot (useAuth.ts:70 getServer) with no reachability check. All API calls fail silently, home page shows skeletons forever, relay WS (derived from same stale URI in storage/server.ts deriveRelayUrl) also fails. Only manual fix is avatar menu -> Change Server, which is not discoverable when everything just looks broken.

## What
On boot (and on sustained fetch failures), ping the stored server connection (e.g. /identity with short timeout). If unreachable, call discoverServers(token) and try to match the stored server by clientIdentifier; if found with different connection URI, transparently update saved server + relay derivation and retry. If discovery fails or no match, surface a clear 'server unreachable' state with a Change Server CTA instead of skeletons.

## Acceptance
- Boot with stale server IP -> app self-heals within seconds when server is discoverable under new IP
- If not discoverable: visible error state with Change Server button, no infinite skeletons
- Relay URL re-derived after server URI update
- Tests for the re-resolve path (mock discoverServers)
close_reason: Closed

title: Popout: snap window to nearest corner on drag-release
description:
In the old (pre-native-player) popout, the floating window snapped to the nearest screen corner. Current native popout is a free-floating window — dragging moves it freely with no snap (nearest_corner only runs at exit to persist the corner, it does NOT reposition the live window). 

Goal: on drag-release of the popout window, compute nearest_corner (already exists in popout.rs) from the live GetWindowRect + work area, then SetWindowPos the window to that corner's corner_origin. 

Notes:
- Needs a window-moved / drag-end signal. Options: tao WindowEvent::Moved (debounced to detect drag-end) on the main window while in popout mode, or a frontend pointerup on a drag handle.
- Reuse existing nearest_corner + corner_origin + work-area resolution in popout.rs.
- Interacts with prexu-6qz (borderless): a borderless popout needs its own drag region anyway; corner-snap should hook the same drag-end.
- Keep it gated to popout mode only (popout=true); do not affect normal windowed playback.
close_reason: wontfix: conflated with old minimize-snap; free-floating drag delivered via 6qz, no corner-snap wanted

title: Borderless popout: drop OS title bar on enter, restore on exit
description: Popout currently keeps the main window's OS title bar (Prexu + min/max/close), so it looks like a shrunk app, not a clean floating mini-player. Add set_decorations(false) on player_enter_popout and set_decorations(true) on player_exit_popout. Needs core:window:allow-set-decorations capability. Native-player single-window design (mpv host is child of main), so this restyles the main window in-place.
notes:
ATTEMPT 1 (set_decorations toggle) REVERTED (commit 72be5c449d) — two regressions confirmed via hands-on:
1. No drag: borderless window has no OS title bar -> no draggable region. Needs an explicit data-tauri-drag-region handle in the popout chrome.
2. ~5s black-but-audio on exit: set_decorations(true) on exit triggers Win32 SWP_FRAMECHANGED non-client recalc that stalls the WebView2 transparent re-arm ([player:transparent] applied fired 3-5s late vs same-second pre-borderless). 
PROPER FIX needs: (a) a drag region, (b) a borderless mechanism that avoids the frame-change repaint (e.g. decorations:false set once at window creation, or a dedicated borderless popout window), NOT a per-popout decorations toggle. Also still wants corner-snap (separate). Scope as its own follow-up, out of wave-3.
close_reason: Closed

title: Spike: tauri-driver feasibility for native player behaviors
description: Timeboxed spike. Investigate whether tauri-driver / WebDriver can drive the real Tauri app on Windows (WebView2) to assert native player STATE (isMinimized, popout, fullscreen flags, geometry persisted) — note it cannot see the mpv surface itself. Report: setup viability on Windows, what's assertable, ROI vs keeping §1-3 manual. Do NOT build a full suite; produce a go/no-go recommendation + minimal proof if viable.
notes: Spike result 2026-05-29: NO-GO (conditional). Tauri v2 WebDriver is supported on Windows (msedgedriver vs WebView2) per v2.tauri.app, but: of 8 manually-tested native behaviors, only 3 surface in the DOM and are WebDriver-assertable — body.player-transparent class (useTransparentWindow.ts), MinimizedPlayer DOM subtree vs full Player (Player.tsx:436), and mini-player.rect in localStorage (mini-rect.ts). All 3 are ALREADY covered by vitest unit tests. The painful behaviors (mpv Win32 surface, window geometry, transparency compositing/desktop-bleed, fullscreen state, DPI) have NO DOM footprint and are fundamentally unassertable by WebDriver. Fragility: msedgedriver must pin to the Evergreen WebView2 runtime or the suite hangs; no headless on Windows; needs a debug --no-bundle build + WebdriverIO harness. ROI: low. Narrow exception: a single IPC-roundtrip smoke test (invoke -> Rust -> event -> DOM reflects) is the only thing tauri-driver would uniquely prove vs vitest's mocked invoke. RECOMMENDATION: keep native player behaviors manual; do not build a tauri-driver suite.
close_reason: Spike complete: NO-GO. Native player behaviors are not WebDriver-assertable (mpv is a Win32 HWND, no DOM); the 3 DOM-assertable signals are already unit-tested; msedgedriver/WebView2 pinning is fragile. Keep §1-3 manual. Full memo in notes.

title: Dashboard: lag between window resize and content reflow filling new size
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-27. When user resizes the main Tauri window while on the dashboard, the dashboard content (sidebar, shelves, cards) lags behind the new window size by ~1-2 seconds. Visible as a gap between the new window edge and where the dashboard content actually ends.

Same root cause family as prexu-0p3 (player chrome reflow lag). WebView2 with transparent: true does not aggressively repaint CSS reflow on window resize; React needs to be nudged to re-render, OR CSS needs GPU compositing hints to force fresh paint.

Candidate fixes:
- ResizeObserver on document.documentElement triggers a setRenderTick / cheap re-render
- window.addEventListener('resize', forceRender) at AppLayout level
- Emit Tauri 'window://resized' event from Rust WindowEvent::Resized, React subscribes and dispatches a state tick
- transform: translateZ(0) + will-change: transform on AppLayout root to force the content onto its own compositor layer that re-flows independently
- Tauri 'window::on_resize' callback inside Rust to forceful WebView2 invalidate

Start with the simplest: window resize listener on the React side that triggers a state update. If insufficient, escalate to ResizeObserver or compositor layer hints.

## Required Skills
react, prexu, anti-fabrication
notes: Implemented: AppLayout adds rAF-throttled window resize listener that triggers setResizeTick state update. Forces React commit per frame during resize, which forces WebView2 repaint. Verify drag-resize on dashboard now snaps content to new size without 1-2s lag.
close_reason: Dashboard reflow lag fixed per user 2026-05-27 ('much better'). Layout read + scrollLeft paint commit on rAF tick. Minor residual lag on aggressive quick-resize accepted as good enough.

title: mini-player: drop resize-handle IPC throttle 50ms -> 33ms (30Hz)
description:

The 50ms floor was inherited from the same '60Hz hard-freezes mpv' rationale as the window-drag sync_geometry throttle in src-tauri/src/player/mod.rs.

The mini-resize path DOES rebuild the mpv vo D3D11 swapchain on every set_geometry, so we can't go to 60Hz. But 30Hz (33ms) is untested and likely fine - the comment specifically called out 60Hz as the freeze threshold.

Acceptance:
- RESIZE_IPC_THROTTLE_MS dropped to 33ms (or measure-confirm a safe value)
- Manual smoke: resize the mini player aggressively, video tracks corner growth/shrink smoothly without mpv stalls
- If 33ms still freezes, file follow-up with measured upper bound

## Required Skills
react, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: RESIZE_IPC_THROTTLE_MS 50 -> 33ms in MiniChrome.tsx. Matches sync_geometry floor proven safe in window-drag path. Smoke test on next tauri dev session.

title: [bug] Pop-out → Minimize first-transition flash exposes desktop under transparent WebView (prexu-a6z.5)
description:
**Reproduction (intermittent, NOT consistently reproducible):**
1. Cold-ish state: app warm but minimize mode not yet exercised this session
2. Enter Pop-out (popout window restores to last persisted corner + size)
3. While in Pop-out, click the Minimize button
4. **Observed (first time only)**: pop-out exits to full main window, minimize takes effect putting video in last-known mini corner (upper-left in this case) — but the rest of the WebView is transparent/clear for a few seconds, exposing the desktop behind it (user saw Discord stream through)
5. **After the dashboard paints**, the transparent area fills with the route UI as expected
6. **Subsequent transitions** in the same session: clean, no flash

**Distinguished from prexu-mto:**
- prexu-mto: clear flash on Resume-from-Beginning when starting playback (initial player mount)
- this: clear flash on a popout-to-minimize transition (mode swap, player already mounted)

Different code path but same underlying class of bug: body.player-transparent is on, AND the WebView's other route content has not yet painted into the non-mini region. With the WebView transparent + nothing painting in the non-mini region, the Win32 mpv host window's pixels show in the mini region only, and the desktop shows through the surrounding empty area.

**Likely cause:**
On pop-out exit, the underlying route (dashboard, item-detail) was offscreen for the duration of pop-out, possibly unmounted or its compositor layer dropped. When minimize takes over, useTransparentWindow keeps body transparent (mini mode uses transparent body too so video shows through the masked region), but the OTHER region needs route content to paint. That paint is slow because:
- React reconciler must rebuild the dashboard tree
- Vite dev-mode HMR overhead
- Could be cold-cached after the popout window swap reset something

**Likely fix candidates:**
A. Keep the underlying route mounted at all times while player is active (already supposed to be true via PlayerOverlay sitting outside route tree — verify it actually is during popout)
B. Don't toggle body.player-transparent until the underlying route has had at least one paint frame; defer the class flip by 1-2 RAF cycles on mode transitions
C. Paint a navy background under the WebView (the existing index.html body navy is the right idea but may not be applying during the transition — verify the body class state during the gap)

**Repro environment:**
Windows, dev build (npm run tauri dev), warm session. User reports only seen ONCE, could not reproduce in subsequent attempts in same session. May be timing-sensitive on:
- Whether Discord (or any topmost partly-transparent window) is layered behind
- Cache state of dashboard data
- Specific corner targeted by mini mode

**Test plan:**
- Try cold app start, enter playback, enter pop-out, then minimize, observe
- Repeat in DevTools network-throttled mode to widen the window
- Add console logging at body.player-transparent toggle vs route mount/paint to confirm sequence

**Suggested Skills:**
- react: useTransparentWindow + PlayerOverlay lifecycle
- tauri: Win32 window-show timing under mode transitions
- testing: this may benefit from a Playwright timing test once reproducible
notes: Approach for full fix (C in 2026-05-27 perf trio): emit player://host-window-ready from end of player_exit_popout and player_enter_minimize commands AFTER their SetWindowPos completes. Update useTransparentWindow to either (a) re-defer on a new player://host-window-busy event fired BEFORE the transition, or (b) the popout/minimize commands explicitly remove the player-transparent body class via emit, then re-add after host-window-ready. Choose simpler path on implementation. Pre-condition: prexu-aqd's drag-fix landed (so we are on the known-working WS_POPUP architecture).
close_reason: Rust commands player_exit_popout + player_enter_minimize now emit busy/ready around their geometry work. useTransparentWindow listens for both: busy drops body.player-transparent so navy body bg fills WebView while dashboard paints, ready re-arms via 2x rAF + sync layout read (uzk paint-commit trick). 12 hook tests + 1427 suite green.

title: [bug] Pop-out forgets which monitor user dragged it to across exit/re-enter (prexu-a6z.3)
description:
**Reproduction:**
1. Main window on monitor 1, enter popout (snaps to monitor 1 corner)
2. Drag popout to a corner of monitor 2 (secondary display)
3. Exit popout (main window restores to its pre-popout position on monitor 1)
4. Re-enter popout
5. **Expected:** popout opens on monitor 2 (where user last had it)
6. **Actual:** popout opens on monitor 1 (where main window currently lives)

**Root cause:**
src-tauri/src/player/commands/popout.rs:40 `current_work_area` uses MonitorFromWindow on the MAIN window's HWND. After exit, main has been restored to its pre-popout geometry (which is on monitor 1), so re-entry's MonitorFromWindow returns monitor 1's work area. The drag-to-monitor-2 gesture is lost.

The recently-shipped prexu-dhg fix persists the CORNER but not the MONITOR.

**Design considerations:**

This is more nuanced than the corner fix. Three sub-decisions to make:

1. **Monitor identifier stability.** HMONITOR handle values change across reboots and monitor disconnect/reconnect. Need a persistent identifier — likely a tuple of (work_area_rect, MONITORINFOEX::szDevice) recorded at exit, then matched at enter against current EnumDisplayMonitors output. If no match found (monitor disconnected), fall back to main window's current monitor.

2. **Conflict with main-window-restore on exit.** Currently exit_popout restores main to its stashed pre-popout geometry — so main snaps back to monitor 1 even if popout is on monitor 2. Two options:
   - **(A) Decouple:** popout has its own remembered monitor + corner; main ALWAYS restores to original. Re-entry: target popout's remembered monitor regardless of where main is.
   - **(B) Follow main:** don't restore main to its pre-popout position if popout has been dragged to a different monitor; leave main wherever the popout landed. Then re-entry's MonitorFromWindow(main) naturally returns the right monitor.
   - (A) is cleaner architecturally but adds a new persistent field. (B) avoids new state but breaks the contract that exit_popout = full restore.

3. **First-run / no-history fallback.** On first popout entry after a fresh install, no persisted monitor exists — use MonitorFromWindow(main) as today.

**Suggested implementation (option A):**
- Add POPOUT_KEY_MONITOR to popout-player.json storing { device_name, work_area: [x, y, w, h] }
- player_exit_popout: enumerate monitors, find the one MonitorFromWindow(main_now_on_M2) maps to, capture its device_name + work_area, persist
- player_enter_popout: load persisted device_name; EnumDisplayMonitors to find a monitor with matching name; if found use ITS work area, else fall back to MonitorFromWindow(main)
- Keep main-window-restore behavior identical to today (it's a contract worth preserving for the maximize+restore loop)

**Suggested Skills:**
- rust: windows crate EnumDisplayMonitors/GetMonitorInfoExW
- tauri: tauri-plugin-store JSON schema
- testing: unit tests for monitor-match logic + fallback path

**Test plan:**
- Manual: drag popout to monitor 2, exit, re-enter — verify monitor 2
- Manual: drag popout to monitor 2, exit, disconnect monitor 2, re-enter — verify graceful fallback to monitor 1
- Manual: same-DPI multi-monitor (currently confirmed working same-DPI from this session)
- Unit: monitor matcher returns correct monitor by device_name, returns None when name not found
- Coordinate with prexu-mhn (different-DPI risk) — same code path

**Out of scope for this issue:**
- DPI-different multi-monitor (separate bug prexu-mhn)
- Per-monitor remembered corner+size (just monitor here; corner + size are global per dhg fix)
notes:
**Confirmed via live logs 2026-05-24 10:54:** drag traces show user moved popout from secondary monitor work area (1602,-2160,3840x2160) into primary monitor (-2238,-2160,3840x2160), ending at (684,-637). Exit_popout's current_work_area correctly reported primary monitor's work area at exit (post-fix from prexu-dhg now detects corner BottomRight of primary correctly). Re-entry then queried MonitorFromWindow on main_hwnd which had been restored back to secondary monitor, so opened BottomRight of secondary. Logs prove the diagnosis: corner persists, monitor does not.

Recommended fix is option A from original design notes (decouple popout monitor from main window position): add POPOUT_KEY_MONITOR persistence using device name from MONITORINFOEX, match on enter via EnumDisplayMonitors with fallback to MonitorFromWindow(main).
close_reason: Closed

title: [risk] Pop-out: untested behavior across monitors with different DPI scaling (prexu-a6z)
description:
**Status:** RISK / untested — not a confirmed regression yet.

**Context:** A6Z.3 multi-monitor was validated for SAME-DPI monitors (popout works correctly when dragged/extended across two 100%-scale displays). DIFFERENT-DPI scenarios were not tested in this session. Historically (pre-native-player) Prexu has had bugs in this area.

If the popout window's GetWindowRect (also physical) is being compared correctly within the same monitor's coordinate space, the math should hold across DPIs.

Filed as backlog because: no concrete repro yet and user does not have a different-DPI setup readily available for testing in this session.

title: [bug] Brief clear-window flash on Resume-from-Beginning when starting playback (prexu-a6z.1)
description:
**Reproduction:**
1. Open Prexu warm (app already running, dashboard visible)
2. Click a poster (e.g. Alice in Borderland) → ResumePopover opens
3. Click 'Resume from Beginning'
4. **Observed**: brief (<1s) flash of clear/transparent window before video starts
5. **Expected**: solid navy or instant video, no transparent flash

**Distinguished from prexu-vs5 (closed):**
vs5 covered the COLD-START path (window first becoming visible after Tauri shell launch) — the app_ready handshake + 3s safety net + body-only opaque navy in index.html addressed that. This new flash happens WARM, in-session, on the playback-start transition. Different code path.

**Likely cause:**
useTransparentWindow toggles `body.player-transparent` ON the moment Player.tsx mounts — before the mpv host Win32 sibling window has been created/positioned behind the WebView. Sequence:
1. ResumePopover.tsx → playerContext.start() → /play/<rk> navigation
2. Player.tsx mounts → useTransparentWindow effect adds `player-transparent` class
3. body becomes alpha=0, WebView reveals what's behind it
4. mpv HostWindow creation runs async (player_init Tauri command)
5. For the ~one frame between #3 and #4, desktop shows through

**Suggested fix:**
Defer the `player-transparent` body class until AFTER the player_init / first-frame event fires, not at component mount. Either:
- Have useTransparentWindow listen for a 'player:host-window-ready' event from Rust and only flip the class then
- OR keep body opaque during a small init window (~250ms) and only flip if not init by then
- OR have the player_init Tauri command paint the mpv host with a navy clear-color BEFORE returning, so even if the WebView is transparent and the host is there, the user sees navy not desktop

**Suggested Skills:**
- react: useTransparentWindow hook lifecycle
- tauri: player_init command sequencing
- rust: mpv host window creation order

**Repro environment:**
Windows, dev build (npm run tauri dev), warm start, after main window already painted dashboard. Logged sub-1s but visually noticeable.
notes: Fixed in 8060d554. useTransparentWindow now defers body.player-transparent class until player://host-window-ready (emitted from PlaybackRestart) or 250ms safety timeout. Rust event flows from events.rs PlaybackRestart branch. 8 vitest cases cover defer + event + timeout + cleanup paths.
close_reason: Resume-from-Beginning flash fixed by deferred transparency.

title: [cleanup] Strip iteration-history + beads-ticket comments from player code (CLAUDE.md compliance sweep)
description:
CLAUDE.md / core comment-discipline rule: 'Don't reference the current task, fix, or callers ("used by X", "added for the Y flow", "handles the case from issue #123"), since those belong in the PR description and rot as the codebase evolves.'

The native-player branch accumulated a lot of inline beads-ticket references and 'previously this did X' narrative as the developer iterated through hard Win32 transparency bugs. The current state is correct but the comments document HOW we got here, not WHY the current invariant holds.

Examples (non-exhaustive):
- Player.tsx L116 — references ensure_init + pb.subtitleStyle + the load case + prexu-id
- Player.tsx L131-141 — five-issue archaeology of mask-based hiding (prexu-s0f / 4ml / ya6 etc.)
- Player.tsx L226-229 — '7il.4: the native path NOW has a SECOND button…'
- Player.tsx L432-447 — handleExitRef rationale referencing prexu-jk0
- AppLayout.tsx L120-128 — 5-step history of hiding mechanism (kfa → ya6 → cay → 9bk → quq)
- src-tauri/src/player/mod.rs — extensive (prexu-buw), (prexu-hhx), (prexu-7il.2 / 7il.7), (prexu-ifj), (prexu-bm0) annotations
- src-tauri/src/player/commands.rs — same density

A reader six months from now does not care which ticket introduced a behavior — git blame answers that. They care about the structural invariant the code is enforcing NOW.

Proposed:
- Sweep all of src/pages/Player.tsx, src/components/AppLayout.tsx, src/components/player/MiniChrome.tsx, src/contexts/PlayerContext.tsx, src-tauri/src/player/*.rs, src/hooks/player/useNativePlayer.ts
- Delete: all (prexu-NNN) parentheticals, all 'previously…' / 'this was changed because…' narrative, all 'NOW does X' temporal language.
- Keep: structural invariants ('Win32 windows are thread-affine, so SetWindowPos from a non-owner thread blocks indefinitely'), non-obvious WHYs ('flushSync because the Dashboard commit takes ~1s and would leak transparent pixels').
- Target ≥ 200 LOC of comment deletion with zero loss of structural understanding.

Acceptance:

title: Cold-start: warm libmpv handle at app launch to eliminate 12s hwdec probe
description:
Concrete trace captured 2026-05-23 02:09:12 (fresh-install run after cargo build). prexu-ps1's diagnostic logs landed at info, so we now have the per-stage timing:

- 02:09:12.x: load_url → mpv init → loadfile sent
- 02:09:34: FileLoaded → player://ready (+22s — cold plex.direct TLS handshake + demuxer parses moov)
- 02:09:46: playback-restart, hwdec-current=d3d11va, time-pos starts moving (+12s — hardware decoder probe + first frame decode)
- Total cold→first-frame: 34s

Dominant: network/demuxer (22s). Secondary: hwdec probe (12s).

The 12s gap is what the user sees as 'stuck at 00:00:00' after the loading screen dismisses — FileLoaded is what dismisses the loading screen, but actual playback waits on hwdec.

Proposed first attempt (from prexu-ps1 closure): warm-init. In the Tauri setup hook, spawn a hidden libmpv handle, set hwdec=auto-safe + vo=gpu-next, run a no-op probe (load a tiny built-in test pattern or ), then keep the handle alive. When user clicks Play, the existing ensure_init reuses the warmed handle; hwdec is already chosen and the D3D11 device is initialised. Expected gain: most of the 12s gap.

Network dominator (22s) is harder — TLS handshake + first byte-range from Plex direct cannot be pre-warmed without knowing the URL. Two paths: (a) DNS pre-resolve to plex.direct on app launch (gets you ~100ms of the 22s), (b) keep an idle keep-alive HTTPS connection to the user's PMS so the TLS session is resumable. Both are speculative — instrument first to confirm where in the 22s the time actually goes (TLS handshake vs byte-range latency vs moov parse).

Acceptance: measured cold→first-frame drops below 15s on a fresh-install run, with the timing logs landed in prexu-ps1 used as the canonical attribution method.

## Required Skills
rust, prexu, tauri, anti-fabrication
notes: Fixed in 9e6aea71. Background thread spawned from app_ready calls PlayerState.ensure_init - creates host window + libmpv handle with hwdec=auto-safe + vo=gpu-next so the user's first Play skips the ~12s probe cost. Idempotent via existing ensure_init mutex check. No frontend changes.

title: [bug] mini-player DPI scale wrong when moving Tauri window between monitors of different DPI
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-22 by user testing (test 33, image #3). Same DPI: PASS. Different DPI: FAIL. After moving the main window to a monitor with a different DPI scale (e.g., 100% → 125% or vice versa), the mini player ends up at the wrong size or position relative to its anchor corner.

prexu-7a2 (already closed) addressed an earlier DPI alignment fix during initial minimize; this is a separate runtime DPI-change scenario — Win32 fires WM_DPICHANGED when the window moves to a different-DPI monitor and the mpv host's set_geometry needs to recompute against the new scale.

Investigation hints:
- Check if there's a WM_DPICHANGED handler in src-tauri/src/player/host_window.rs or main.rs that re-runs sync_geometry with the new scale factor.
- Check if the React side (Player.tsx miniContainer) reads window.devicePixelRatio at the right time. Logical px in miniRect vs physical px in Rust set_geometry — the bridge must rescale on DPI change.
- Tauri's WindowEvent::ScaleFactorChanged is the cross-platform hook for this.

Will be largely fixed by the mpv-as-child-window architectural change (separate bd issue). In the meantime, two quicker mitigations: 1) reduce the throttle from 50ms to ~16ms during active drag, 2) ensure pending_geometry is always applied after each drag completes (not dropped on next event). Either patch is a stopgap until the parenting refactor lands.

title: [cleanup] make mpv host a child of Tauri main window so drag is free; current sync_geometry throttle visibly lags
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-21 from pop-out drag testing (image #41). Today the mpv host is a sibling Win32 window of the WebView, repositioned on each Tauri WindowEvent::Resized/Moved via sync_geometry (50ms throttle). During an active drag Windows fires hundreds of move events but most are dropped by the throttle; mpv visibly lags behind the popout window during drag and sometimes ends up partially outside the new window position.

Image #42 shows the symptom: mpv stuck on a single frame for several seconds because a critical sync_geometry call was dropped.

Architectural fix: make mpv host a CHILD of the Tauri main window (not a sibling). Win32 child windows automatically follow their parent's translation — no event-driven sync needed for pure drag. Resize still needs sync_geometry but only fires on actual resize, not on every move.

Transparency compositing should still work: mpv as a child behind the WebView2 (z-order child position), WebView2 still has transparent pixels in the mpv area, OS composites through.

Bigger architectural change than the context split. Needs careful testing on hwnd parenting, mpv's d3d11 swapchain behaviour, fullscreen transitions (prexu-dsh already noted dont resize mpv from host wndproc).

WindowEvent::Moved arm dropped - Win32 moves child for free. apply_host_topmost becomes no-op (children inherit parent topmost). cargo check + cargo test 29/29 + tsc + vitest 1401 all pass. title: [cleanup] split PlayerContext so isMinimized/popout changes do not cascade through Dashboard
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-21 as the architectural cleanup behind prexu-pa4. Today PlayerContext holds session + play + stop + isMinimized + minimize + restoreFromMinimize + isPopOut in a single memoized value. When any one changes, all consumers re-render including Dashboard's deeply-nested tree which makes the React commit take ~1s on the Home route.

Split into focused contexts:
- SessionContext: session, play, stop, replaceRatingKey, updateSession
- MinimizeContext: isMinimized, minimize, restoreFromMinimize
- (maybe) PopOutContext: pop-out state if needed

After the split, isMinimized changes only invalidate AppLayout + Player.tsx (~5 components vs ~50 today). React commit drops to sub-frame; flushSync becomes unnecessary; transitions feel instant.

~200 line refactor. Best done in its own session.

title: [bug] player chrome layout lags WebView resize after popout exit until auto-hide cycle
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-21 during prexu-70y/prexu-wow testing. After exiting pop-out (or any abrupt window resize), the React chrome (PlayerControls top + bottom bars) stays positioned at the previous-smaller window size for up to 3 seconds even though the Tauri main window and mpv host have both already expanded. The chrome auto-hide timer fires → opacity transition forces a re-paint → mouse-over re-shows the chrome at the correct full size.

Not a state bug — React.isMinimized=false correctly, mpv host is at full WebView client area correctly. The chrome's CSS (position: fixed; inset: 0) simply hasn't re-flowed against the new WebView viewport yet. WebView2 + tao window resize → CSS reflow is lazy on the WebView side.

Cosmetic, briefly-visible, self-correcting. Filed as P3.

Candidate fixes:
- WindowEvent::Resized on Rust emits a player://resize event; Player.tsx subscribes + setState to force re-render.
- ResizeObserver on document.documentElement in Player.tsx triggers a setRenderTick to nudge layout.
- transform: translateZ(0) on the chrome container so it sits on its own compositor layer that re-flows independently.

title: Dashboard/library: per-item incremental rendering instead of whole-shelf skeletons
description:
Better UX inspired by the official Plex client. Currently App.tsx's pre-fetch in AppRoutes() awaits getRecentlyAddedBySection for each section as a batch via Promise.all-style and only writes to the dashboard cache when the whole batch resolves; the dashboard shelf shows a unified row-level skeleton until all items arrive. The official Plex client renders items incrementally — each tile is its own loading state and pops in as it streams.

Surfaced 2026-05-21 by user during prexu-7il testing. Per-item rendering would noticeably improve perceived speed on first load and slow networks (e.g., remote Plex).

Refactor sketch:
- Each dashboard shelf component owns its own items array (state) and renders tiles individually with per-tile skeletons.
- Backing fetch produces items as they resolve (Promise.allSettled per item OR streaming-style array growth) rather than blocking on the whole batch.
- Consider migrating shelf data to React 19 use() + Suspense boundaries per row for cleaner UX integration.
- Apply same approach to /library/:sectionId LibraryView for scroll-down-before-loaded responsiveness.

Scope: dashboard shelves + LibraryView. Out of scope: detail pages, search.

title: Revamp PostPlayScreen — richer 'Playing Next' for shows + playlists
description:
**Today**: src/components/player/PostPlayScreen.tsx is intentionally minimal. The overlay shows:
- 'PLAYING NEXT' label
- 160x110 thumbnail with a small SVG countdown ring + number
- Title (h2), subtitle (e.g. 'S01E02 — Episode Title'), duration in minutes
- AUTO PLAY ON checkbox, 'Play Now' button, 'Stop' button
- Esc/Enter shortcuts

**User feedback (2026-05-04)**: it's barebones. Wants a richer 'Playing Next' experience for episodes in a show and items in a playlist.

## Goals (to be refined)

Things worth considering when scoping the revamp:
- **Episode context**: for shows, show 'S2 E5' badge prominently, episode synopsis (1-2 lines truncated), maybe air date.
- **Show/playlist progress**: 'Episode 5 of 13 in Season 2' or 'Item 3 of 7 in Playlist Name'.
- **Larger thumbnail** with backdrop or hero-style framing — small 160x110 thumb feels lost on a TV-ish app.
- **Up-next queue preview**: optional thin row underneath showing the 2-3 items after the next one (subtle, scrollable).
- **Skip to a different next**: ability to override the auto-selected next item (e.g. skip an episode, jump to specific item in playlist).
- **Better autoplay countdown affordance**: bigger ring or progress bar; current 48x48 SVG is small.
- **Star rating / Plex audience score** for context (Plex's own UX shows this).
- **End-of-season / end-of-show edge cases**: when next item is from a different show or season, surface that ('Starting Season 3' / 'New Show: ...').
- **Watched indicator**: if the user has already seen the next item, mark it.
- **Keyboard shortcuts displayed inline**: small 'Enter to play • Esc to stop • → for queue' hint.

## Constraints

- PostPlayScreen is currently shown via Player.tsx based on hasNextItem (gated on itemType === 'episode'). The prexu-9yn issue is open to extend this to movies in playlists; coordinate scope.
- The player route may become an overlay (prexu-kfa); PostPlay should keep working in either architecture.
- Keep the 10s countdown default; respect the new persisted autoPlayEnabled preference (added in 909bc8f).

## Acceptance (to refine when picked up)

- Visual design is closer in feel to Plex's PlayingNext screen (richer card, more context, larger affordances).
- For TV episodes: clearly shows season/episode/series context + episode synopsis.
- For playlist items: clearly shows position in playlist + playlist name.
- Auto-play countdown is more visible (size, contrast, or progress bar).
- Existing shortcuts (Enter/Esc) and autoplay toggle still work; no regression to handlePostPlayNext / handlePostPlayStop.
- Vitest + tsc green.

## Out of scope

- Skip-to-arbitrary-queue-item UI (could be a separate follow-up if desired).
- Animation polish (slide-in, parallax) — nice to have, not essential.

Added 20 colocated tests. Preserves existing autoplay countdown, Enter/Esc shortcuts, and onPlayNext/onStop contract. Optional rich-context props degrade gracefully when parent does not pass them.

title: Post-Player-unmount: AppLayout's empty Outlet shows body navy until destination paints
description:
Symptom (2026-05-03 user report): after clicking Stop, user sees a 1-2 second navy paint before the destination page renders. Confirmed via dev log:

- handleExit invokes player_unload — returns in 8ms (per c70a7d0 fix).
- Player cleanup useEffect runs ~1s later, judging by the 'cleanup: stopping timeline, unloading' log timestamp.
- Total navy duration matches user perception of 1-2s.

Root cause: src/components/AppLayout.tsx:178-183 has no background on the container — it's a flex column with header (var(--bg-secondary)) + sidebar + Outlet area. The Outlet area inherits the body bg (which Player.tsx's useLayoutEffect cleanup paints navy on unmount, per the 'native-player-fullscreen-host-window-geometry-sync-confirmed' memory). Until the destination route's lazy() chunk resolves AND the component renders content into the Outlet, the user sees navy from body bg.

Lazy routes (App.tsx:14-31): Player, Dashboard, LibraryView, ItemDetail, etc. All wrapped in a single <Suspense fallback={<LoadingScreen />}>. Even when chunks are pre-loaded, React + React Router commit takes a noticeable moment in dev.

Possible fixes (not yet picked):
1. Give AppLayout's main/Outlet area an explicit background that matches body (--bg-primary), so the body navy is no longer 'visible' through it. Simplest, addresses symptom not cause.
2. Replace the body-bg dance entirely. Player.tsx sets body transparent on mount because mpv host window shows through; restore on unmount. Alternative: render an absolutely-positioned navy panel underneath Player that only Player toggles, leaving body bg alone.
3. Pre-paint a Suspense fallback in the Outlet so the user sees a 'returning to library' state during the transition.

Acceptance: clicking Stop on the player returns to the previous page within ~200ms of perceived navy fill, OR shows a clear loading state instead of static navy.

## Required Skills
